Auto-regressive models for code generation from natural language have a similar limitation: they do not easily allow reconsidering earlier tokens generated. We introduce CodeFusion, a pre-trained diffusion code generation model that addresses this limitation by iteratively denoising a complete program conditioned on the encoded natural language. We evaluate CodeFusion on the task of natural language to code generation for Bash, Python, and Microsoft Excel conditional formatting (CF) rules. Experiments show that CodeFusion (75M parameters) performs on par with state-of-the-art auto-regressive systems (350M-175B parameters) in top-1 accuracy and outperforms them in top-3 and top-5 accuracy due to its better balance in diversity versus quality.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.17680","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/SinghCG0NV23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.716"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/SinghCG0NV23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.716","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":8,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"AR 代码生成模型无法回头修改已生成的 token，这对需要全局一致的代码（变量名、括号匹配、格式规则）是硬伤。作者想用 diffusion 的迭代去噪来支持'反复改写整段程序'这种生成方式。","importance":"对 diffusion LM 范式是一篇有说服力的 case study：在代码这种结构性强、局部错一处整体就挂的领域，non-AR 的迭代 refinement 是有天然优势的。75M 参数打平 350M–175B 的 AR 系统这个数字如果能站得住，会让'AR 必然碾压 diffusion'的默认假设松动一些。但 72M 与 175B 的对比需要小心看 tokenizer、数据、解码预算等是否公平。","technical_approach":"Encoder-decoder 结构：自然语言经 encoder 编码作为条件，decoder 端在连续 embedding 空间做 diffusion，最后用一个 classification head 把去噪后的 embedding 映射回离散 token。预训练阶段用 unsupervised code denoising，再在 NL→code 对上 fine-tune。与纯离散 diffusion（D3PM 系）不同，它走 embedding-space continuous diffusion，目的是让梯度传导更顺畅、也更容易复用 Transformer 架构。","experiments":"三个任务：Bash、Python（CoNaLa 风格）、Excel 条件格式规则。CodeFusion 75M 在 top-1 上对标 350M–175B 的 AR baseline（GPT-3、CodeT5、StarCoder 等），在 top-3/top-5 多样性上优势更明显——这点符合 diffusion 的直觉：同一 prompt 去噪出的候选更分散。","authors_note":"Microsoft 团队（Sumit Gulwani 组，长期做 program synthesis / PROSE）。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Autoregressive code generators can't revise earlier tokens, which hurts code where global consistency (brackets, naming, format rules) matters. The paper uses diffusion's iterative denoising to allow rewriting the whole program across steps.","importance":"A decent case study for diffusion LMs on a domain where non-AR refinement is naturally suited. If the 75M-vs-175B top-1 parity holds up, it weakens the default 'AR strictly dominates diffusion' view. Caveat: the comparison spans very different tokenizers, data, and decoding budgets, so read the headline number carefully.","technical_approach":"Encoder-decoder: NL encoded as condition; decoder runs continuous diffusion in embedding space, then a classification head maps denoised embeddings back to discrete tokens. Pretrained with unsupervised code denoising, then NL→code fine-tuning. Continuous-embedding diffusion (not D3PM-style discrete) for cleaner gradients and easy Transformer reuse.","experiments":"Bash, Python, Excel CF rules. 75M CodeFusion matches 350M–175B AR baselines (GPT-3, CodeT5, StarCoder) in top-1, with clearer wins in top-3/top-5 diversity — consistent with diffusion producing more varied candidates per prompt.","authors_note":"Microsoft (Sumit Gulwani's program-synthesis line, PROSE lineage)."},"tags":["diffusion-lm","code-generation","non-ar-lm"],"notable_authors":["Sumit Gulwani"]},{"id":"2207.10551","title":"Scaling Laws vs Model Architectures: How does Inductive Bias Influence Scaling?","authors":["Yi Tay","Mostafa Dehghani","Samira Abnar","Hyung Won Chung","William Fedus","Jinfeng Rao","Sharan Narang","Vinh Q. Tran","Dani Yogatama","Donald Metzler"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"There have been a lot of interest in the scaling properties of Transformer models. However, not much has been done on the front of investigating the effect of scaling properties of different inductive biases and model architectures. Do model architectures scale differently? If so, how does inductive bias affect scaling behaviour? How does this influence upstream (pretraining) and downstream (transfer)? This paper conducts a systematic study of scaling behaviour of ten diverse model architectures such as Transformers, Switch Transformers, Universal Transformers, Dynamic convolutions, Performers, and recently proposed MLP-Mixers. Via extensive experiments, we show that (1) architecture is an indeed an important consideration when performing scaling and (2) the best performing model can fluctuate at different scales. We believe that the findings outlined in this work has significant implications to how model architectures are currently evaluated in the community.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2207.10551","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/Tay0ACFRN0YM23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.FINDINGS-EMNLP.825"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/Tay0ACFRN0YM23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.findings-emnlp.825","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":8,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{},"summary_en":{},"tags":["scaling-law","architecture","inductive-bias","pretraining"]},{"id":"ThawaniGZP23","title":"Learn Your Tokens: Word-Pooled Tokenization for Language Modeling","authors":["Avijit Thawani","Saurabh Ghanekar","Xiaoyuan Zhu","Jay Pujara"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/ThawaniGZP23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.FINDINGS-EMNLP.662"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/ThawaniGZP23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.findings-emnlp.662","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":8,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{},"summary_en":{},"tags":["tokenizer","language-modeling","subword","pretraining"]},{"id":"2305.14160","title":"Label Words are Anchors: An Information Flow Perspective for Understanding In-Context Learning","authors":["Lean Wang","Lei Li","Damai Dai","Deli Chen","Hao Zhou","Fandong Meng","Jie Zhou","Xu Sun"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"In-context learning (ICL) emerges as a promising capability of large language models (LLMs) by providing them with demonstration examples to perform diverse tasks. However, the underlying mechanism of how LLMs learn from the provided context remains under-explored. In this paper, we investigate the working mechanism of ICL through an information flow lens. Our findings reveal that label words in the demonstration examples function as anchors: (1) semantic information aggregates into label word representations during the shallow computation layers' processing; (2) the consolidated information in label words serves as a reference for LLMs' final predictions. Based on these insights, we introduce an anchor re-weighting method to improve ICL performance, a demonstration compression technique to expedite inference, and an analysis framework for diagnosing ICL errors in GPT2-XL. The promising applications of our findings again validate the uncovered ICL working mechanism and pave the way for future studies.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.14160","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/WangLDCZMZS23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.609"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/WangLDCZMZS23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.609","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":8,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解释 in-context learning 到底是如何工作的，重点不是描述现象，而是定位信息在层间如何流动。过去对 ICL 机制的讨论很多，但常停留在“像隐式梯度更新”或“像模式匹配”的类比，缺少能指导 prompt 设计和压缩的具体可操作解释。","importance":"它的重要性在于提供了一个可操作的机制性观点：示例中的 label words 是锚点。对 pretrain 和机制分析的价值很直接，因为这把 ICL 的一部分行为从黑箱现象变成了可干预对象，进一步导出 re-weighting 与 demonstration compression 这样的具体方法，而不仅是事后解释。","technical_approach":"核心方法是从 information flow 视角分析 ICL，并提出‘label words are anchors’这一机制假设。作者的结论是：浅层计算时，语义信息会向 label word 的表示聚合；后续预测再把这些被聚合过的信息当作参照。这和把所有 token 视为同等示例载体的看法不同。基于这个观察，作者做了两件事：一是对 anchor token 重新加权以提升 ICL；二是利用 anchor 性质压缩 demonstrations。设计有效的原因很明确：如果关键决策主要绑定在标签词周围，那就应优先保留并强化这些位置，而不是平均处理整个示例。","experiments":"从摘要能确认的事实是，论文既有机制分析，也提出了 anchor re-weighting 和 demonstration compression 两个应用。摘要没有给出模型规模、所测任务、具体提升幅度或层分析细节，因此不能写具体数字。能稳妥总结的是：作者声称浅层聚合、后层引用这一两阶段信息流模式，并且该模式可被利用来做性能提升和上下文压缩，而不是只停留在解释层面。","authors_note":"作者包含 Lei Li、Hao Zhou。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper asks a mechanistic question: how does information actually flow during in-context learning? Prior discussions often rely on broad analogies such as implicit learning or pattern matching, but those views are hard to turn into concrete interventions for prompt design or context compression.","importance":"Its contribution is mechanistic and actionable. The claim that label words act as anchors turns part of ICL from a vague emergent effect into something that can be inspected and manipulated. However, recent studies have established that MoE models are inherently parameter-inefficient as the improvement in performance diminishes with an increasing number of experts. We hypothesize this parameter inefficiency is a result of all experts having equal capacity, which may not adequately meet the varying complexity requirements of different tokens or tasks. The interaction between pretraining data and task data is commonly assumed to determine this variance: a task with data that is more similar to a model's pretraining data is assumed to be easier for that model. We test whether distributional and example-specific similarity measures (embedding-, token- and model-based) correlate with language model performance through a large-scale comparison of the Pile and C4 pretraining datasets with downstream benchmarks. Prior half-sparse schemes replace layers uniformly and ignore that layers play different roles.","importance":"A useful datapoint for the long-context efficiency literature: keep full attention only where it matters, cheapen the rest. Directly informs compute-budget allocation in long-context pretraining.","technical_approach":"Mixed-layer attention. Keep full attention in a small subset of layers ('attend more') and use cheap local/sparse attention elsewhere. The non-trivial bit is picking which layers to keep—driven by analysis of attention entropy / effective receptive field, the 'global' layers cluster in the mid-to-late stack. Training uses the mixed stack, so inference inherits the speedup.","experiments":"On long-text LM and classification/retrieval, matches full-attention quality with ~50% or fewer full-attention layers, cutting FLOPs substantially. To help address this, we develop two modes of evaluation for natural language explanations that claim individual neurons represent a concept in a text input. In the *observational mode*, we evaluate claims that a neuron a activates on all and only input strings that refer to a concept picked out by the proposed explanation E. In the *intervention mode*, we construe E as a claim that neuron a is a causal mediator of the concept denoted by E. We apply our framework to the GPT-4-generated explanations of GPT-2 XL neurons of Bills et al. (2023) and show that even the most confident explanations have high error rates and little to no causal efficacy. We close the paper by critically assessing whether natural language is a good choice for explanations and whether neurons are the best level of analysis.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.10312","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/blackboxnlp/HuangGDWP23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.BLACKBOXNLP-1.24"},"_dblp_key":"conf/blackboxnlp/HuangGDWP23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.blackboxnlp-1.24","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"解决用大模型（如 GPT-4）自动生成自然语言来解释神经元功能的评估难题，指出当前缺乏严谨的验证手段来判断这些解释是否忠实于神经元的真实行为。","importance":"评估级贡献。给 OpenAI 的自动可解释性工作泼了冷水，建立了一套严格的证伪框架，对后续依赖 LLM 做机制分析的研究设定了极高的验证标准。","technical_approach":"提出两种评估模式：观察模式（神经元是否仅在解释提及的概念上激活）和干预模式（神经元是否是该概念的因果中介）。将自然语言解释转化为可计算的逻辑断言进行严格测试。","experiments":"在 OpenAI 提供的 GPT-4 解释 GPT-2 XL 神经元的数据集上测试，发现即使是置信度最高的解释，也存在极高的错误率（不符合观察或干预一致性）。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Addresses the evaluation challenge of using LLMs (like GPT-4) to automatically generate natural language explanations for neuron functions, noting the lack of rigorous methods to verify their faithfulness.","importance":"Evaluation contribution. In particular, we show that probing for “my colour” vs. “opponent’s colour” may be a simple yet powerful way to interpret the model’s internal state. Prior work mostly showed accuracy gains from prompting with CoT, but did not test whether the generated reasoning is correct or whether those intermediate tokens causally affect the final answer.","importance":"This is a mechanism paper rather than a new reasoning method. Its main contribution is to move the discussion from correlation to causal use of CoT, which matters for how we interpret process supervision, rationale distillation, and CoT-based filtering; if CoT is not causally used, many current post-training signals would be weaker than they look.","technical_approach":"The method combines correctness analysis with causal abstraction. First, it measures how strongly correct CoTs align with correct answers. Then it uses intervention-based causal abstraction to test whether changing intermediate CoT tokens changes the final answer in the way the reasoning trace predicts. This is stronger than comparing prompts with and without CoT, because it probes whether the content of the reasoning path matters. The focus on arithmetic word problems is deliberate: they offer cleaner latent variables and more checkable reasoning chains, though that also narrows the scope of the conclusion.","experiments":"From the abstract, the key result is that for CoT-prompted LLMs, correct answers are strongly correlated with correct CoTs, and intermediate CoT tokens have measurable causal impact on final answers. A straightforward fix is to scale the model up, but that increases both training and inference cost.","importance":"This is an architecture and capacity-allocation contribution. First, train a larger multilingual model to reduce interference. Then, for each language, search for a lottery-ticket subnetwork that matches the full model’s performance for that language. The important design choice is to avoid hard language partitioning from the start; instead, the model first learns in a shared over-parameterized space, and only later are the useful per-language parameter subsets extracted. We investigate whether better sample efficiency and the better generalization capability of models pretrained with masked language modeling can be attributed to the semantic similarity encoded in the pretraining data's distributional property. Via a synthetic dataset, our analysis suggests that distributional property indeed leads to the better sample efficiency of pretrained masked language models, but does not fully explain the generalization capability. We also conduct analyses over two real-world datasets and demonstrate that the distributional property does not explain the generalization ability of pretrained natural language models either. Our results illustrate our limited understanding of model pretraining and provide future research directions.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.16261","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/ChiangY23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.637"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/ChiangY23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.637","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文的核心结论是：分布假说只能部分解释 masked language model 预训练的收益，尤其解释不了其泛化能力。过去很多分析默认 MLM 的优势主要来自词在相似上下文中出现，从而学到语义接近性；作者要检验这个解释到底够不够。","importance":"这是对预训练目标理解的直接修正。它的重要性不在于推出新 objective，而在于告诉我们：如果把 MLM 的好处简单归因于 distributional similarity，就会误判预训练信号的来源。对 pretrain 研究的含义是，目标函数为什么能带来迁移与泛化，可能还依赖结构归纳偏置、条件化方式或优化路径，而不是仅靠词共现统计。","technical_approach":"方法上是分析型工作。作者先用 synthetic dataset 做可控实验，隔离分布性质与泛化行为；再在两个真实数据集上复核结论。这个设计有两个优点。第一，合成数据允许精确控制 distributional property，因此能更清楚地区分样本效率和泛化。第二，真实数据验证避免结论只停留在 toy setting。论文得到的判断是：distributional property 确实解释了 pretrained MLM 更好的 sample efficiency，但不足以解释 generalization。也就是说，‘学到相似词在相似上下文中出现’能让模型更快学，但不能完整说明为什么它能跨分布或跨任务泛化。","experiments":"关键实验事实在摘要里很清楚。第一，在 synthetic 数据上，分布性质可以解释样本效率提升，但不能完全解释泛化。第二，在两个真实世界数据集上，作者同样发现 distributional property 不能解释自然语言模型的泛化能力。摘要没有给出模型规模、具体任务、指标和数值，因此无法安全写数字。最值得关注的 ablation 是 synthetic 数据如何控制分布相似性，以及在去掉这些因素后泛化是否仍保留。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper makes a sharp claim: the distributional hypothesis explains only part of the benefit of masked language model pretraining, and it does not fully account for generalization. Many analyses implicitly assume that MLM works mainly because words appearing in similar contexts acquire similar semantics. The authors test whether that explanation is sufficient.","importance":"This is an important corrective for how we think about pretraining objectives. Its value is not a new objective, but a clearer statement of what the standard explanation misses. For pretraining research, the implication is that MLM gains may depend on inductive bias, conditioning structure, or optimization effects beyond distributional statistics alone.","technical_approach":"The paper uses controlled analysis rather than proposing a new model. It first constructs a synthetic dataset where distributional properties can be manipulated cleanly, then checks whether the same conclusions hold on two real-world datasets. This design separates sample efficiency from generalization, which is the key distinction. The result is nuanced: distributional structure does explain why pretrained MLMs learn more sample efficiently, but it does not fully explain why they generalize better. That means contextual co-occurrence is part of the story, not the whole story.","experiments":"The core empirical findings are stated in the abstract. On synthetic data, distributional properties explain the sample-efficiency benefit of MLM pretraining but fail to fully explain generalization. The same mismatch appears on two real-world datasets. The abstract does not include model sizes, task names, or metrics, so no numerical claims should be added. University of Hong Kong","ByteDance"],"abstract":"Diffusion models have gained prominence in generating high-quality sequences of text. Nevertheless, current approaches predominantly represent discrete text within a continuous diffusion space, which incurs substantial computational overhead during training and results in slower sampling speeds. In this paper, we introduce a soft absorbing state that facilitates the diffusion model in learning to reconstruct discrete mutations based on the underlying Gaussian space, thereby enhancing its capacity to recover conditional signals. During the sampling phase, we employ state-of-the-art ODE solvers within the continuous space to expedite the sampling process. Comprehensive experimental evaluations reveal that our proposed method effectively accelerates the training convergence by 4x and generates samples of similar quality 800x faster, rendering it significantly closer to practical application. \\footnote{The code is released at \\url{https://github.com/Shark-NLP/DiffuSeq}","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.05793","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/GongLF0K23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.FINDINGS-EMNLP.660"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/GongLF0K23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.findings-emnlp.660","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"文本扩散模型在连续空间建模离散文本时训练慢、采样慢。DiffuSeq-v2 要解决的是如何在保持生成质量的同时大幅加速训练收敛和推理速度。","importance":"方法级贡献，直接推进 diffusion LM 的实用性。对 pretrain 研究的意义：diffusion LM 是挑战 AR 范式的重要方向，但训练/推理效率一直是瓶颈；本文将训练加速 4x、采样加速 800x，显著缩小了与 AR 模型的效率差距。","technical_approach":"引入 soft absorbing state，在连续高斯扩散空间中让模型学习重建离散突变（discrete mutations），从而增强对条件信号的恢复能力。这是在连续和离散文本空间之间架桥的关键设计。采样阶段使用先进的 ODE solver 在连续空间中加速。与原始 DiffuSeq 相比，核心区别在于不再纯粹在连续空间操作，而是利用离散状态信息引导扩散过程。","experiments":"训练收敛加速约 4x，采样速度提升约 800x（摘要截断，具体 benchmark 和指标需查全文）。在 seq2seq 任务上进行了全面评估，生成质量与加速效果均有提升。","authors_note":"Lingpeng Kong 来自香港大学，在结构化生成和扩散模型方面有持续产出。DiffuSeq 是该团队的前序工作。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Text diffusion models suffer from slow training and sampling when modeling discrete text in continuous space. token 的表示成本太高，而简单截断又会丢掉关键远程信息。此前常见做法是在固定窗口、segment recurrence 或 memory token 之间折中，但都没有真正按'哪些过去内容值得记'来选择缓存。","importance":"它的贡献属于方法级和效率级。对 pretrain 的启示是，长上下文能力不一定只能靠更长训练窗口和更大显存，也可以通过选择性记忆改变有效上下文分配；这与训练时的 token budget、推理时 KV-cache 开销、以及 memory-augmented LM 设计直接相关。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 selective cache：不是缓存所有历史状态，而是有选择地保留对未来预测最有用的部分。与 Transformer-XL 式固定保留过去 segment 相比，它强调 selection；与检索式记忆不同，它仍在标准语言建模框架内工作。合理推断其关键设计包括：基于注意力、surprisal、位置或学习到的打分函数挑选历史表示写入缓存，并在后续 token 预测时访问该缓存。这样设计有效，是因为长程依赖通常稀疏，不是所有历史 token 都值得保留。","experiments":"没有摘要，无法确认所用数据集和 perplexity 改善幅度。按标题判断，实验应在长文档语言建模基准上，对比固定上下文窗口、Transformer-XL、Compressive Transformer 或其他 memory baselines，主要指标是 perplexity 与单位计算/内存成本。最有价值的 ablation 应包括：缓存预算变化、选择策略差异，以及性能是否主要来自少数关键远程 token。无可靠数字可写。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles a classic long-context trade-off in language modeling: full history helps, but caching everything is expensive, while naive truncation discards useful distant context. Earlier approaches such as fixed windows or segment recurrence keep history mechanically rather than deciding what is actually worth remembering.","importance":"This is a method and efficiency contribution. For pretraining, the main implication is that long-range capability may come not only from larger context windows, but also from better memory allocation. That has direct relevance to token budgets during training and to KV-cache cost at inference.","technical_approach":"The key idea is a selective cache that stores only a subset of historical states judged useful for future prediction. Unlike Transformer-XL-style recurrence, which keeps past segments by rule, this approach emphasizes selection; unlike external retrieval, it remains within the language-modeling pipeline. The likely mechanism is to score past tokens or states using attention, surprise, position, or a learned utility signal, then retain only the highest-value memory entries. This design is sensible because long-range dependencies in text are sparse rather than uniformly distributed.","experiments":"The abstract is missing, so dataset names and perplexity gains cannot be stated reliably. No numeric details are safely recoverable.","authors_note":""},"tags":["long-context","cache","lm"]},{"id":"2310.16076","title":"Practical Computational Power of Linear Transformers and Their Recurrent and Self-Referential Extensions","authors":["Kazuki Irie","Róbert Csordás","Jürgen Schmidhuber"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"Recent studies of the computational power of recurrent neural networks (RNNs) reveal a hierarchy of RNN architectures, given real-time and finite-precision assumptions. Here we study auto-regressive Transformers with linearised attention, a.k.a. linear Transformers (LTs) or Fast Weight Programmers (FWPs). LTs are special in the sense that they are equivalent to RNN-like sequence processors with a fixed-size state, while they can also be expressed as the now-popular self-attention networks. We show that many well-known results for the standard Transformer directly transfer to LTs/FWPs. Our formal language recognition experiments demonstrate how recently proposed FWP extensions such as recurrent FWPs and self-referential weight matrices successfully overcome certain limitations of the LT, e.g., allowing for generalisation on the parity problem. Our code is public.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.16076","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/IrieCS23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.588"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/IrieCS23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.588","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作的结论是：线性 Transformer 在有限精度、实时计算假设下的计算能力可以被严格刻画，而且其局限与扩展路径都比工程界常见说法更清楚。过去大家常把 linear attention 当成'更快但近似更差的 attention'，较少从形式语言与状态容量角度分析它究竟能算什么、不能算什么。","importance":"这是一篇范式分析级工作。对 pretrain 的意义不在于直接提升某个 benchmark，而在于澄清一类高效架构的能力边界：线性 Transformer 本质上等价于固定状态大小的 RNN/Fast Weight Programmer，因此它的表达瓶颈不是简单的近似误差，而是状态受限；相应地，递归扩展和自指权重能否补足能力，关系到长上下文、高效注意力和替代架构的可行性判断。","technical_approach":"核心方法是把 auto-regressive linear Transformer 形式化为固定大小状态的序列处理器，并把标准 Transformer 的若干计算能力结论迁移到 LT/FWP 上，再分析其递归和自指扩展。论文进一步研究 recurrent FWP 和 self-referential weight matrices 这些扩展为何能突破基础 LT 的限制。与经验性架构论文不同，这里不是先堆 benchmark，而是先给计算模型，再用形式语言识别实验验证能力层级。这样设计有效，因为对这类架构，很多关键差异在长程可计算性上，普通困惑度实验不一定能暴露。","experiments":"已知摘要明确提到 formal language recognition experiments。可可靠总结的实验结论是：基础 LT/FWP 受固定状态约束，在某些形式语言上存在能力限制；而 recurrent FWP 和 self-referential extensions 能成功克服其中一部分限制。摘要没有给出具体语言集合、模型规模或准确率数字，因此不能编造。最值得注意的观察是，这类实验不是为了追求 SOTA，而是用可控任务验证理论层级是否在实际训练中可见。","authors_note":"作者包括 Jürgen Schmidhuber。团队长期代表性方向包括 Fast Weight Programmers、RNN 计算能力分析、线性/高效 Transformer 理论。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper argues that the practical computational power of linear Transformers can be characterized much more precisely than the usual 'faster but weaker attention' narrative suggests. Linear Transformers are effectively fixed-size-state RNN-like processors, so their limits are tied to state capacity rather than merely approximation quality. That matters for long-context modeling, efficient attention, and whether such architectures can serve as serious alternatives to standard Transformers.","technical_approach":"The paper formalizes autoregressive linear Transformers as fixed-state sequence processors, equivalent to Fast Weight Programmers, and transfers several known computational results from standard Transformers to this setting. Smith","Mike Lewis"],"institutions":["University of Washington","Allen Institute for AI"],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/PressZMSSL23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.FINDINGS-EMNLP.378"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/PressZMSSL23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.findings-emnlp.378","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"LLM 在需要多步组合推理的问题上存在'组合性差距'（compositionality gap）：模型能正确回答每个子问题，但无法将子答案组合得到最终答案。本文量化了这一差距并提出 self-ask 方法来缩小它。","importance":"范式级启示。组合性差距直接揭示了 LLM 推理能力的一个结构性弱点——不是知识不够，而是组合能力不足。对 pretrain 研究者来说，这暗示单纯增加数据量或模型规模可能无法解决组合推理问题，需要在训练目标或推理策略上做文章。Self-ask 是 CoT 的一个重要变体。","technical_approach":"首先定义并量化组合性差距：在多跳问答中，模型能答对所有子问题但答错组合问题的比例。然后提出 self-ask：让模型在回答前先自己提出并回答子问题（类似递归分解），可选地结合搜索引擎获取子答案。关键洞察是显式分解比隐式 CoT 更能帮助模型完成组合推理。","experiments":"在 Compositional Celebrities 和 2WikiMultiHopQA 等多跳 QA 数据集上测试。组合性差距在 GPT-3（text-davinci-002）上约 40%+。Self-ask 将多跳 QA 准确率显著提升，尤其结合搜索引擎时效果最好。随模型规模增大，差距有所缩小但未消失。","authors_note":"Ofir Press（University of Washington / AI2），此前以 ALiBi 位置编码闻名。Noah A. Smith 和 Sewon Min 也是 NLP 领域的知名研究者。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"LLMs exhibit a 'compositionality gap': they can answer individual sub-questions correctly but fail to compose sub-answers into a correct final answer for multi-hop questions. This paper measures this gap and proposes self-ask to narrow it.","importance":"A paradigm-level insight. The compositionality gap reveals a structural weakness in LLM reasoning—it's not about lacking knowledge but about failing to compose. For pretrain researchers, this suggests that scaling data or parameters alone may not solve compositional reasoning; training objectives or inference strategies need rethinking. Self-ask is an influential CoT variant.","technical_approach":"First defines and quantifies the compositionality gap: the fraction of multi-hop questions where the model answers all sub-questions correctly but the composed question incorrectly. Then proposes self-ask: the model explicitly generates and answers sub-questions before the final answer, optionally using a search engine for sub-answers. The key insight is that explicit decomposition outperforms implicit chain-of-thought for compositional reasoning.","experiments":"Tested on Compositional Celebrities and 2WikiMultiHopQA. Smith","Sewon Min"]},{"id":"Ren0Z23","title":"Context Compression for Auto-regressive Transformers with Sentinel Tokens","authors":["Siyu Ren","Qi Jia","Kenny Q. Zhu"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/Ren0Z23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.794"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/Ren0Z23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.794","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：在不改动自回归生成范式的前提下，把长上下文中的冗余历史压缩掉，从而降低 Transformer 的计算和显存成本。以往长上下文通常靠稀疏注意力、外部记忆或截断历史来规避二次复杂度，但这些方法要么改模型较重，要么直接损失生成所需的细粒度条件信息。","importance":"这是一篇方法级的效率工作，价值在于给自回归 Transformer 提供了一种训练内生的上下文压缩接口。对 pretrain 的启示不是提出新范式，而是说明可以用专门的哨兵 token 学会把历史信息汇聚到少量位置，再在推理时只保留这些位置，作为 KV-cache 和长上下文训练之间的折中方案。","technical_approach":"核心方法是引入 sentinel tokens 作为可学习的信息汇聚点，让模型在序列内部主动做上下文压缩。具体做法应是周期性插入或追加少量哨兵 token，使其通过注意力吸收邻近片段的信息；随后在后续层或后续时间步优先依赖这些压缩表示，而不是保留全部历史 token。它和直接做 pooling 的区别在于压缩位置仍然处于标准 token 序列中，因此兼容原生 Transformer；它和外部记忆的区别在于记忆是端到端随语言建模目标一起学出来的，而不是另加检索模块。代价是压缩比越高，越容易丢掉局部词法细节，因此更适合存在重复或主题稳定的长上下文，而不一定适合每个 token 都关键的精细生成场景。","experiments":"从题目可判断，实验重点应是长序列语言建模或生成任务中的速度—质量权衡，但当前给定信息没有摘要、表格或数字，因此不能可靠写出模型规模、数据规模和具体提升幅度。可以合理预期论文会对比标准 Transformer 或其他上下文压缩基线，并报告在相近困惑度或生成质量下的计算节省；若有消融，最关键的应是哨兵数量、插入频率和压缩位置对性能的影响。由于缺少原始摘要，这里不对数值结果做推断。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles a practical bottleneck: how to compress long autoregressive context without abandoning standard left-to-right generation. Prior work usually sidestepped the quadratic cost with sparse attention, truncation, or external memory, but those choices either change the model substantially or drop token-level information that generation may still need.","importance":"This is a method-level efficiency paper with a clear pretraining relevance: it proposes an in-model interface for context compression rather than a separate memory system. The main takeaway for pretrain research is that a language model may learn to funnel useful history into a small set of dedicated tokens, giving a middle ground between full-context training and aggressive cache pruning.","technical_approach":"The core idea is to add sentinel tokens that act as learned aggregation slots for surrounding context. Instead of pooling hidden states outside the sequence, the model compresses information into a few ordinary sequence positions that remain compatible with the vanilla Transformer stack. Relative to external memory, the compressed representation is trained end-to-end under the language modeling objective. Relative to naive downsampling, it preserves a differentiable path for the model to decide what to keep. The trade-off is straightforward: higher compression reduces compute more, but it also risks losing local lexical detail, so the method is likely strongest when history is redundant or semantically stable.","experiments":"From the title, the key empirical focus is likely the quality-versus-efficiency trade-off on long-context language modeling or generation. Several studies have explored text diffusion models with different structures and applied them to various tasks, including named entity recognition and summarization. However, there exists a notable disparity between the\"easy-first\"text generation process of current diffusion models and the\"keyword-first\"natural text generation process of humans, which has received limited attention. To bridge this gap, we propose InfoDiffusion, a non-autoregressive text diffusion model. Our approach introduces a\"keyinfo-first\"generation strategy and incorporates a noise schedule based on the amount of text information. In addition, InfoDiffusion combines self-conditioning with a newly proposed partially noising model structure. Experimental results show that InfoDiffusion outperforms the baseline model in terms of generation quality and diversity, as well as exhibiting higher sampling efficiency.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.11976","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/WangLL23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.FINDINGS-EMNLP.919"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/WangLL23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.findings-emnlp.919","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是文本 diffusion 生成顺序与人类写作过程不匹配的问题。现有很多非自回归文本 diffusion 模型偏向 easy-first 逐步恢复，而人类往往先确定关键信息再补细节；这种顺序错配会让模型在关键信息保真和生成效率之间都吃亏。","importance":"它的重要性在于对 diffusion LM 的生成范式提出了一个更细的修正：不仅要讨论离散扩散能不能做文本，还要讨论‘先生成什么’。对新型语言建模研究的启发是，噪声调度不只是优化细节，它实际上决定了模型学到的内容优先级，因此直接影响非 AR 文本生成的质量与可控性。","technical_approach":"InfoDiffusion 的核心是一个 information-entropy aware 的离散文本 diffusion 过程。它提出 keyinfo-first 生成策略，并按文本信息量设计噪声调度；同时结合 self-conditioning 和一种新的 partially noising 训练策略。和已有 easy-first diffusion 文本生成相比，关键区别是把 token 的恢复顺序从‘噪声容易去掉的先恢复’改成‘信息承载更关键的先恢复’。这样设计的直觉很强：如果关键词先定下来，后续句法和补充内容会更容易收敛。代价是关键性估计本身若不稳，可能带来错误放大。","experiments":"从摘要可确认，论文是一个 non-autoregressive text diffusion model，并明确比较了 easy-first 与其 keyinfo-first 路线。摘要没有给出数据集、模型规模、baseline、指标和提升数字，因此不能填具体结果。可以明确记录的实验要点是：作者不只改 noise schedule，还把 self-conditioning 与部分加噪训练联动起来，这通常意味着他们关注训练-采样一致性和迭代稳定性，而不是只做单点技巧替换。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a mismatch between how current text diffusion models generate and how informative text is typically composed. Many non-autoregressive diffusion models follow an easy-first recovery pattern, whereas humans often settle key information first and fill in details later. That mismatch can hurt both content fidelity and generation behavior.","importance":"This matters because it refines the discussion around diffusion LMs beyond the usual AR-vs-NAR framing. Its main idea is a keyinfo-first generation strategy, paired with a noise schedule based on the amount of textual information. It also combines self-conditioning with a newly proposed partially noising training strategy. The key departure from prior easy-first diffusion methods is that recovery order is driven by information importance rather than denoising convenience. The motivation is simple: if high-value content is established early, the remaining syntactic and local content decisions become easier. The risk is that unstable importance estimation can propagate mistakes early in the denoising chain.","experiments":"The abstract confirms that this is a non-autoregressive text diffusion model and that it explicitly contrasts keyinfo-first generation with the common easy-first pattern. Sheffield"],"abstract":"Scaling pre-trained language models has resulted in large performance gains in various natural language processing tasks but comes with a large cost in memory requirements. Inspired by the position embeddings in transformers, we aim to simplify and reduce the memory footprint of the multi-head attention (MHA) mechanism. We propose an alternative module that uses only a single shared projection matrix and multiple head embeddings (MHE), i.e. one per head. We empirically demonstrate that our MHE attention is substantially more memory efficient compared to alternative attention mechanisms while achieving high predictive performance retention ratio to vanilla MHA on several downstream tasks. MHE attention only requires a negligible fraction of additional parameters ($3nd$, where $n$ is the number of attention heads and $d$ the size of the head embeddings) compared to a single-head attention, while MHA requires $(3n^2-3n)d^2-3nd$ additional parameters.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.07911","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/XueA23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.FINDINGS-EMNLP.695"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/XueA23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.findings-emnlp.695","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"Multi-Head Attention (MHA) 的参数量随 head 数线性增长，每个 head 都有独立的 Q/K/V 投影矩阵，这在大模型中占据显著内存。问题是能否用更少的参数实现接近 MHA 的效果。","importance":"方法级贡献，属于注意力机制的参数效率优化。与 MQA (Multi-Query Attention) / GQA (Grouped-Query Attention) 是同一赛道的不同思路。对 pretrain 研究的启示：用 head embedding 替代独立投影矩阵是一种极端压缩方案，额外参数仅 3nd（n 为 head 数，d 为维度），但需要验证在大规模预训练中是否保持性能。","technical_approach":"核心思路受位置编码启发：所有 head 共享一个投影矩阵，每个 head 仅通过一个小的 head embedding 来区分。这样 Q/K/V 的参数从 O(n·d²) 降到 O(d²+n·d)。与 MQA 的区别在于 MQA 共享 K/V 但保留独立 Q，而 MHE 更激进地共享所有投影，仅靠 embedding 区分 head。trade-off 是表达能力可能下降。","experiments":"在多个下游任务上评估，MHE 注意力在大幅减少参数的同时保持了较高的性能保留率（相对 vanilla MHA）。具体数值未在摘要中给出。关键观察是额外参数量极小（3nd），但性能保留率需要在更大规模模型上验证。","authors_note":"Huiyin Xue, Nikolaos Aletras，来自 University of Sheffield。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"MHA parameters scale linearly with head count due to independent Q/K/V projections per head, consuming significant memory. The question is whether near-MHA performance can be achieved with far fewer parameters.","importance":"Method-level contribution in attention parameter efficiency, in the same space as MQA/GQA but with a more aggressive compression approach. For pretrain research: the idea of replacing per-head projections with shared projection + head embeddings is an extreme compression point worth testing at scale.","technical_approach":"Inspired by position embeddings: all heads share a single projection matrix, differentiated only by small per-head embeddings (MHE). This reduces Q/K/V parameters from O(n·d²) to O(d²+n·d). More aggressive than MQA (which shares K/V only)—MHE shares everything and relies solely on embeddings for head specialization. The trade-off is potential expressiveness loss.","experiments":"Evaluated on several downstream tasks. MHE attention achieves high performance retention relative to vanilla MHA with substantially fewer parameters (only 3nd additional parameters). Specific numbers not in the abstract. Scalability to large-scale pretraining remains to be validated.","authors_note":"Huiyin Xue, Nikolaos Aletras, University of Sheffield."},"tags":["attention-optimization","multi-head-attention","parameter-efficiency","architecture"]},{"id":"YangS0R23","title":"Bootstrapping Small &amp; High Performance Language Models with Unmasking-Removal Training Policy","authors":["Yahan Yang","Elior Sulem","Insup Lee","Dan Roth"],"institutions":["University of Pennsylvania"],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/YangS0R23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.30"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/YangS0R23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.30","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"如何训练小而高性能的语言模型。提出一种 unmasking-removal 训练策略来 bootstrap 小模型的能力。","importance":"方法级贡献。对 pretrain 研究的启示在于：小模型的训练策略可能需要与大模型不同的课程设计。如果 unmasking-removal 能有效提升小模型性能，这对资源受限场景下的预训练有实际价值。","technical_approach":"摘要缺失，无法提供详细技术描述。根据标题推断：训练过程中逐步 unmask 并移除已学会的内容，形成一种课程学习策略，让小模型更高效地利用训练数据。","experiments":"摘要缺失，无法提供实验细节。","authors_note":"Dan Roth（University of Pennsylvania / Amazon，NLP 领域资深研究者，ACL Fellow）。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"How to train small yet high-performing language models, using an unmasking-removal training policy to bootstrap small model capabilities.","importance":"Method-level contribution. However, multiple underlying factors are associated with an MLM such as its model size, size of the training data, training objectives, the domain from which pretraining data is sampled, tokenization, and languages present in the pretrained corpora, to name a few. It remains unclear as to which of those factors influence social biases that are learned by MLMs. To study the relationship between model factors and the social biases learned by an MLM, as well as the downstream task performance of the model, we conduct a comprehensive study over 39 pretrained MLMs covering different model sizes, training objectives, tokenization methods, training data domains and languages. Our results shed light on important factors often neglected in prior literature, such as tokenization or model Naively applying discrete diffusion to these units ignores the continuous semantic structure and degrades quality.","importance":"A method-level contribution showing that running diffusion in continuous embedding space rather than discrete token space is more effective for speech unit generation. The insight—that discrete tokens with latent continuous structure benefit from continuous-space diffusion—is relevant to speech LM and discrete diffusion research more broadly.","technical_approach":"DiffS2UT maps discrete speech units into continuous embeddings, applies Gaussian diffusion in that space, and trains a denoiser to recover the clean embeddings, which are then mapped back to discrete units. This preserves semantic proximity between units (unlike discrete diffusion where all tokens are equidistant) and enables parallel generation. The key design choice is continuous-space diffusion over discrete tokens, trading the simplicity of discrete diffusion for better gradient-based denoising and semantic preservation.","experiments":"The abstract does not provide specific numbers. Experiments presumably compare against autoregressive S2UT and discrete diffusion baselines on standard S2ST benchmarks. Foster"],"institutions":["University of Chicago","Argonne National Laboratory"],"abstract":"Answering multi-hop reasoning questions requires retrieving and synthesizing information from diverse sources. Large Language Models (LLMs) struggle to perform such reasoning consistently. Here we propose an approach to pinpoint and rectify multi-hop reasoning failures through targeted memory injections on LLM attention heads. First, we analyze the per-layer activations of GPT-2 models in response to single and multi-hop prompts. We then propose a mechanism that allows users to inject pertinent prompt-specific information, which we refer to as “memories,” at critical LLM locations during inference. By thus enabling the LLM to incorporate additional relevant information during inference, we enhance the quality of multi-hop prompt completions. We show empirically that a simple, efficient, and targeted memory injection into a key attention layer can often increase the probability of the desired next token in multi-hop tasks, by up to 424%.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.05605","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/blackboxnlp/SakarvadiaAKGHBCF23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.BLACKBOXNLP-1.26"},"_dblp_key":"conf/blackboxnlp/SakarvadiaAKGHBCF23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.blackboxnlp-1.26","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"解决 LLM 在多跳推理时因无法有效整合分散信息而失败的问题，提出一种无需微调、在推理期直接干预注意力头的方法。","importance":"方法级贡献。展示了在 inference 阶段通过激活干预（Activation Intervention）来增强推理能力的可行性，与近期流行的基于内部状态干预的对齐/增强方法思路一致。","technical_approach":"首先分析 GPT-2 在单跳和多跳 prompt 下的逐层激活差异，定位关键注意力头。然后在推理时，将特定 prompt 相关的外部信息（称为“memories”）直接注入到这些关键位置的激活中。","experiments":"在 GPT-2 模型上证明，这种简单、高效的免微调（tuning-free）注入机制能够显著提升多跳推理 prompt 的完成质量。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Addresses LLM failures in multi-hop reasoning caused by the inability to effectively synthesize scattered information, proposing a tuning-free method to intervene directly in attention heads during inference.","importance":"Methodological contribution. Demonstrates the feasibility of enhancing reasoning capabilities through activation intervention during inference, aligning with recent trends in internal state intervention for alignment and augmentation.","technical_approach":"First analyzes layer-wise activation differences in GPT-2 under single- and multi-hop prompts to locate critical attention heads. Then, during inference, injects prompt-specific external information ('memories') directly into the activations at these critical locations.","experiments":"Proved on GPT-2 models that this simple, efficient, tuning-free injection mechanism significantly improves the completion quality of multi-hop reasoning prompts."},"tags":["multi-hop","interpretability","attention"]},{"id":"SinghMARG23","title":"Explaining Data Patterns in Natural Language with Language Models","authors":["Chandan Singh","John X. Morris","Jyoti Aneja","Alexander M. Rush","Jianfeng Gao"],"institutions":["Microsoft Research","Cornell University"],"abstract":"Large language models (LLMs) have displayed an impressive ability to harness natural language to perform complex tasks. We explore whether we can leverage this ability to find and explain patterns in data. Specifically, given a pre-trained LLM and data examples, we apply interpretable autoprompting (iPrompt) to generate a natural language string explaining the data. iPrompt iteratively generates explanations with an LLM and reranks them based on their performance when used as a prompt. Experiments on a wide range of datasets, from synthetic mathematics to natural language understanding, show that iPrompt can yield meaningful insights by accurately finding dataset explanations that are human-interpretable. Moreover, iPrompt is reasonably efficient, as it does not require access to model gradients and works with relatively small models (e.g. ~6 billion parameters rather than >=100 billion). Finally, experiments with scientific datasets show the potential for iPrompt to aid in scientific discovery.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/blackboxnlp/SinghMARG23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.BLACKBOXNLP-1.3"},"_dblp_key":"conf/blackboxnlp/SinghMARG23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.blackboxnlp-1.3","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"解决如何利用 LLM 自动发现并用自然语言解释数据集中的潜在模式，且无需访问模型梯度。","importance":"方法级贡献。探索了 LLM 作为“数据分析师/模式提取器”的潜力，提供了一种可解释的 prompt 优化范式，对数据清洗和指令合成有启发。","technical_approach":"提出 iPrompt（可解释自动提示）。给定数据样本，利用 LLM 迭代生成自然语言解释，并根据这些解释作为 prompt 在数据上的预测性能进行重新排序（reranking）。","experiments":"在合成数学和 NLU 数据集上测试。iPrompt 能够准确找到人类可理解的数据集解释，且由于是黑盒操作，计算效率较高。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Addresses how to leverage LLMs to automatically discover and explain latent patterns in datasets using natural language, without requiring access to model gradients.","importance":"Methodological contribution. Existing solutions often extend the context window or summarize the input, but the former is expensive and the latter can rewrite or distort information.","importance":"This is a systems-oriented contribution with relevance to serving and long-context training pipelines. The main lesson is that long-context inefficiency is often an input-efficiency problem, not only a model-capacity problem. If selective pruning preserves quality, it can reduce inference cost and inform upstream data packing or retrieval filtering.","technical_approach":"The proposed method, Selective Context, identifies and removes redundant parts of the input to make the context more compact. The key design choice is to prefer selective retention over abstractive rewriting, which keeps the original wording and reduces semantic drift. Compared with retrieval, it compresses the provided context itself rather than fetching external evidence. This should work when relevant information is sparse, but the trade-off is obvious: tasks requiring broad aggregation or global discourse cues may be less tolerant to pruning.","experiments":"From the abstract, the method is tested on arXiv papers, news articles, and long conversations, across summarization, QA, and response generation, and improves inference efficiency while maintaining task performance. Inspired by insights from cognitive science, our task-adaptive tokenizer samples variable segmentations from multiple outcomes, with sampling probabilities optimized based on task-specific data. We introduce a strategy for building a specialized vocabulary and introduce a vocabulary merging protocol that allows for the integration of task-specific tokens into the pre-trained model's tokenization step. Through extensive experiments on psychological question-answering tasks in both Chinese and English, we find that our task-adaptive tokenization approach brings a significant improvement in generation performance while using up to 60% fewer tokens. Preliminary experiments point to promising results when using our tokenization approach with very large language models.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.05317","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/0003DSJHM23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.944"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/0003DSJHM23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.944","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文解决的是一个常被忽略但很实际的问题：预训练模型的通用 tokenizer 未必适合特定长文本生成任务，尤其在心理健康问答这类高频术语、固定表达和长句并存的场景里，低效切分会拉长序列、稀释语义片段，并放大生成难度。过去通常默认 tokenizer 固定不动，只在模型或数据上做适配，因此 tokenization 这一层的任务不匹配常被忽略。","importance":"这项工作的意义在于把 tokenizer 重新拉回训练/生成效率讨论中，属于数据表示层面的改进。对 pretrain 研究的启示很直接：tokenizer 不是中性的前处理，而是决定序列长度、语义边界和训练难度的核心部件；即便不重训大模型，针对任务做轻量 tokenization 适配也可能带来可观收益。这一点对长上下文、跨语言和领域适配都值得重视。","technical_approach":"作者提出 task-adaptive tokenization。核心做法是让 tokenizer 从多个可变切分结果中采样，并根据任务数据优化这些切分的采样概率；同时构建任务专用词表，并通过 vocabulary merging protocol 将这些 task-specific tokens 接入已有预训练模型的 tokenization 流程。这个设计不是完全推倒重练 tokenizer，而是在兼容原模型词表的前提下做局部增补和分词重配。这样做有效，因为它同时解决了两件事：一是把任务中高价值片段压成更短、更稳定的 token 序列；二是避免彻底更换词表导致 embedding 和模型输入接口失配。边界是，这种收益依赖任务分布足够集中；若任务非常开放，专用词表可能泛化有限。","experiments":"摘要提供了几个关键事实：论文在中英文心理健康问答的长文本生成任务上做实验；task-adaptive tokenization 能显著提升生成表现，同时使用更少 token。摘要没有给出模型规模、数据规模、主 baseline、自动指标或 token 降幅，因此不能补数字。可以确认的价值点是，该方法同时改善质量和长度效率，而不是单纯通过增加词表获得更大模型容量。","authors_note":"作者中 Minlie Huang 是较知名作者，长期从事中文对话、生成模型与大模型研究。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses an often overlooked mismatch: a generic pretrained tokenizer may be suboptimal for specific long-form generation tasks. The main lesson is that tokenization is not a neutral preprocessing step; it shapes sequence length, semantic boundaries, and optimization difficulty. Even without retraining a foundation model from scratch, task-adaptive tokenization can change downstream generation quality and efficiency.","technical_approach":"The proposed task-adaptive tokenizer samples from multiple possible segmentations and optimizes the sampling probabilities using task-specific data. Prior work often treats sensitivity as a symptom of instability, but that does not tell us whether it is merely harmful or partly tied to the mechanism that enables ICL in the first place.","importance":"This is a mechanism paper rather than a new pretraining method, but it matters for how we interpret context use in LMs. If sensitivity and accuracy are systematically linked, then prompt robustness should not be evaluated in isolation, and some forms of sensitivity may reflect genuine use of demonstrations rather than simple brittleness.","technical_approach":"From the title, the contribution is a controlled analysis of the sensitivity-accuracy relationship. McKeown","He He"]},{"id":"2310.13620","title":"Bridging Information-Theoretic and Geometric Compression in Language Models","authors":["Emily Cheng","Corentin Kervadec","Marco Baroni"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"For a language model (LM) to faithfully model human language, it must compress vast, potentially infinite information into relatively few dimensions. We propose analyzing compression in (pre-trained) LMs from two points of view: geometric and information-theoretic. We demonstrate that the two views are highly correlated, such that the intrinsic geometric dimension of linguistic data predicts their coding length under the LM. We then show that, in turn, high compression of a linguistic dataset predicts rapid adaptation to that dataset, confirming that being able to compress linguistic information is an important part of successful LM performance. As a practical byproduct of our analysis, we evaluate a battery of intrinsic dimension estimators for the first time on linguistic data, showing that only some encapsulate the relationship between information-theoretic compression, geometric compression, and ease-of-adaptation.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.13620","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/ChengKB23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.762"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/ChengKB23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.762","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文的核心结论是：语言模型里的“压缩”可以同时从几何维度和信息论编码长度两种角度理解，而且二者高度相关。过去这两个视角通常分开讨论——一个看表示空间的内在维度，一个看模型给数据分配的负对数似然——作者想回答它们是不是在衡量同一件更底层的能力。","importance":"这是机制与表征层面的工作，价值在于把两个原本分裂的分析框架接起来。对 pretrain 研究的启示很直接：如果内在几何维度能预测 coding length，而 coding length 又预测适应速度，那么‘会压缩的数据更容易被模型快速吸收’就不只是直觉，而可能成为分析数据难度、继续预训练收益和 domain adaptation 难度的通用标尺。","technical_approach":"方法上它不是提出新 LM，而是做一套桥接分析：先在预训练 LM 上同时测 linguistic dataset 的几何内在维度和信息论压缩率，再看二者相关性；随后检验高压缩数据是否更易快速适配。关键设计有两点。第一，作者把 coding length 视作模型对数据结构利用程度的可观测量，而不是只看表面 perplexity。第二，他们系统比较了一组 intrinsic dimension estimator 在语言数据上的表现，试图找到对 NLP 更稳健的估计器。这样设计有效，因为语言分布高维、稀疏且强结构化，通用几何工具未必能直接迁移。边界在于，这类相关性分析更擅长解释趋势，不一定能说明具体训练机制。","experiments":"论文最重要的实验事实有三条。第一，语言数据的几何内在维度与在 LM 下的 coding length 高度相关，说明几何压缩和信息论压缩并非彼此独立。第二，压缩率高的数据集往往适应更快，支持“压缩能力是成功 LM 表现的一部分”。第三，作者首次在语言数据上系统评估多种 intrinsic dimension estimator，并指出其中有些估计器更可靠。给定摘要没有具体模型规模、数据规模和相关系数数字，因此不能补写数值；但从贡献看，重点是相关性强、趋势稳定，而不是刷新某个 benchmark。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The central claim is that compression in language models can be understood through two lenses—geometric intrinsic dimension and information-theoretic coding length—and that these two views are strongly aligned. These perspectives are usually studied separately, so the paper asks whether they are in fact measuring the same underlying capability of the model.","importance":"This is a mechanistic and representational contribution rather than a new training recipe. Its relevance to pretraining is that it turns a vague intuition into a usable analysis tool: if intrinsic dimension predicts coding length, and coding length predicts adaptation speed, then compressibility becomes a practical way to reason about dataset difficulty, continual pretraining benefit, and domain adaptation ease.","technical_approach":"The paper builds a bridge rather than a new LM. It measures both the intrinsic geometric dimension of linguistic datasets and their coding length under pretrained LMs, then studies the correlation between them. It further tests whether highly compressible datasets adapt faster. A useful design choice is to treat coding length as a model-based observable of how much structure the LM can exploit, instead of relying only on perplexity. Another key piece is the systematic evaluation of several intrinsic-dimension estimators on linguistic data, because off-the-shelf geometric estimators are not guaranteed to behave well in high-dimensional, highly structured language representations. The limitation is standard for this type of work: strong correlations explain trends, but they do not fully identify the causal training mechanism.","experiments":"The main empirical facts are clear from the abstract. First, geometric intrinsic dimension strongly correlates with coding length under the LM. Second, datasets that compress better tend to allow faster adaptation. Third, the paper benchmarks a set of intrinsic-dimension estimators on linguistic data and identifies estimators that are more suitable in this setting. The abstract does not provide model sizes, dataset counts, or correlation coefficients, so no numeric gains should be inferred.","authors_note":""},"tags":["representation","compression","geometry"],"notable_authors":["Emily Cheng","Corentin Kervadec","Marco Baroni"]},{"id":"2305.14788","title":"Adapting Language Models to Compress Contexts","authors":["Alexis Chevalier","Alexander Wettig","Anirudh Ajith","Danqi Chen"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$2a","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.14788","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/ChevalierWAC23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.232"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/ChevalierWAC23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.232","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文解决的是 Transformer 上下文窗口有限、长文处理成本过高的问题，而且它不满足于简单截断或检索拼接，而是希望把长历史压缩成模型可继续利用的内部状态。过去做长上下文通常要么直接扩窗，算力和显存代价线性到二次增长；要么外接摘要器，但摘要文本会丢信息且训练目标不一致。","importance":"这是一个很典型的 pretrain↔serving 接口工作，但方法本身也接近新的建模能力：把预训练 LM 适配成能“写工作记忆”的 AutoCompressor。它的重要性在于，若无监督地学到可复用的 summary vectors，就有机会把超长上下文变成软提示状态，而不是每次重读原文；这直接关系到长上下文预训练、memory 机制和推理成本优化。","technical_approach":"核心方法是把长文分段处理，并为每一段生成 compact summary vectors；之后模型在做语言建模时，把所有先前段的 summary vectors 当作 soft prompts 读入。它是对现成预训练 OPT 的适配，而不是从头设计新架构。关键机制有两个。第一，训练目标是无监督的下一词预测，因此 summary vector 被迫保留对未来 token 预测最有用的信息，而不是做人类可读摘要。第二，历史被压成固定数量向量，计算从‘重读全部 token’变成‘读少量记忆槽’，这就是它省成本的来源。它舍弃的是逐 token 精确访问能力，因此适合保留全局语义与主题，不一定擅长细粒度定位。","experiments":"实验上，作者在 OPT 模型上微调，最长训练序列到 30,720 tokens。核心结果有两类。第一，AutoCompressor 能利用长上下文改善 perplexity，说明 summary vectors 确实携带了对语言建模有用的历史信息。第二，在 in-context learning 评估里，它先压缩大量示例再作为软提示使用，表明这种记忆形式不只服务于 PPL，也能服务于 few-shot 条件化。摘要没有给出具体参数量、PPL 降幅或相对 baseline 数字，因此不能补写；但最值得关注的 ablation 通常会是 summary vector 数量、分段长度以及压缩比对性能的影响。","authors_note":"作者包含 Danqi Chen。若按作者阵容看，相关团队在检索增强、长上下文建模和高效 LM 使用上长期有积累。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a practical limitation of Transformer LMs: finite context windows make long-document processing expensive and fragile. Instead of simply extending the window or using textual summaries, it asks whether a pretrained LM can be adapted to write reusable internal memory states that preserve predictive information from long contexts.","importance":"This is highly relevant at the pretrain-serving boundary, but it also changes the modeling interface itself: the LM becomes an AutoCompressor that carries forward learned summary vectors rather than rereading raw tokens. That matters for long-context pretraining and memory-augmented modeling, because it offers a way to trade exact token access for cheaper persistent context.","technical_approach":"The method processes long documents in segments and produces compact summary vectors for each segment. During language modeling, summary vectors from all previous segments are fed back as soft prompts. This is an adaptation of pretrained OPT models, not a new model family from scratch. The design works because the objective remains unsupervised next-token prediction, so the summary vectors are optimized to retain whatever history is most useful for predicting future tokens, not to be human-readable. The compute win comes from replacing repeated access to many old tokens with access to a small fixed memory. The trade-off is clear: compressed memory should retain coarse predictive structure, but fine-grained retrieval of exact earlier details becomes harder.","experiments":"The paper fine-tunes OPT models on sequences up to 30,720 tokens. The main findings are that AutoCompressors use long contexts to improve perplexity, and that the compressed vectors also help in in-context learning when many demonstrations need to be packed into limited space. The abstract does not state parameter counts, baseline names, or numeric improvements, so those should not be invented. The most informative ablations are likely memory size, segment size, and compression ratio.","authors_note":"The author list includes Danqi Chen, whose group is well known for retrieval-augmented and efficient language modeling work."},"tags":["context-compression","long-context"],"notable_authors":["Alexis Chevalier","Alexander Wettig","Anirudh Ajith","Danqi Chen"]},{"id":"2305.03796","title":"Transformer Working Memory Enables Regular Language Reasoning And Natural Language Length Extrapolation","authors":["Ta-Chung Chi","Ting-Han Fan","Alexander Rudnicky","Peter J. Ramadge"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"Unlike recurrent models, conventional wisdom has it that Transformers cannot perfectly model regular languages. Inspired by the notion of working memory, we propose a new Transformer variant named RegularGPT. With its novel combination of Weight-Sharing, Adaptive-Depth, and Sliding-Dilated-Attention, RegularGPT constructs working memory along the depth dimension, thereby enabling efficient and successful modeling of regular languages such as PARITY. We further test RegularGPT on the task of natural language length extrapolation and surprisingly find that it rediscovers the local windowed attention effect deemed necessary in prior work for length extrapolation.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.03796","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/ChiFRR23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.FINDINGS-EMNLP.397"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/ChiFRR23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.findings-emnlp.397","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文的核心问题是：标准 Transformer 被普遍认为不能严格建模正则语言，也难做稳定的长度外推。过去的回避方式通常是引入 recurrence、外部状态机，或者接受在训练长度之外性能崩溃；作者想证明，Transformer 只要有合适的‘工作记忆’结构，未必做不到。","importance":"它的重要性在于对架构能力边界提出了直接修正。对 pretrain 研究来说，这不是在做纯理论题，而是在问：长度泛化和算法性模式学习，究竟是 Transformer 范式不行，还是现有深度/注意力组织方式不对。如果后者成立，那么很多长上下文和 reasoning 能力问题可以通过记忆组织来改，而不一定要彻底换范式。","technical_approach":"作者提出 RegularGPT，核心不是单一 trick，而是三件事的组合：Weight-Sharing、Adaptive-Depth、Sliding-Dilated-Attention。它们共同的作用是沿深度维构造 working memory，让网络在层间逐步传递和更新有限状态，而不是只在 token 维做一次性全局混合。Weight sharing 让不同深度像重复执行同一状态转移；adaptive depth 让不同输入复杂度用不同计算步数；sliding-dilated attention 则在局部窗口和稀疏跳连之间折中，既保局部性又扩大感受野。和传统 Transformer 相比，它更像把‘时间上的 recurrence’折到了‘深度上的迭代’里。代价是训练与实现更复杂，且对通用大规模语言建模是否同样收益还需要证据。","experiments":"实验上，论文声称 RegularGPT 能成功建模 PARITY 等正则语言，并在自然语言长度外推上表现出优势。更有意思的一点是，模型在自然语言外推时重新发现了 prior work 认为必要的 local windowed attention 效应，这说明工作记忆结构可能是促成长度外推的更一般机制。摘要没有给出参数量、数据集、具体长度区间和提升数字，因此不能硬写数字；但关键观察已足够明确：它在形式语言和自然语言外推两侧都做了验证，而不是只在 toy task 上成立。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper challenges a common belief: standard Transformers are often considered unable to model regular languages perfectly and unreliable at length extrapolation. Prior work usually sidesteps this with recurrence, external memory, or by accepting failure beyond training lengths. The authors ask whether the limitation is architectural in principle, or mostly a matter of how memory is organized.","importance":"This matters because it reframes a capability boundary. For pretraining research, the implication is that some failures in algorithmic pattern learning and length generalization may come from the default Transformer organization rather than from the Transformer paradigm itself. If so, memory structure becomes a concrete design lever.","technical_approach":"The proposed model, RegularGPT, combines three ingredients: weight sharing, adaptive depth, and sliding-dilated attention. Together they create a form of working memory along the depth axis, so the network can iteratively update internal state rather than relying only on one-pass token mixing. Weight sharing makes depth behave more like repeated state transitions. Adaptive depth allocates more computation to harder inputs. Sliding-dilated attention balances local attention with sparse longer-range access. Relative to a standard Transformer, this effectively moves some recurrence into depth. The trade-off is added architectural and optimization complexity, and it is not yet obvious how well the same design scales to mainstream large-scale pretraining.","experiments":"Empirically, the paper reports success on regular-language tasks such as PARITY and also on natural-language length extrapolation. A particularly interesting result is that the model rediscovers the local windowed-attention effect that prior work argued was necessary for length extrapolation, suggesting that working-memory structure may be the more general explanation. The abstract provides no parameter counts, datasets, or exact gains, so the summary should stay qualitative.","authors_note":""},"tags":["architecture","length-extrapolation","attention"],"notable_authors":["Ta-Chung Chi","Ting-Han Fan","Alexander Rudnicky","Peter J. Ramadge"]},{"id":"2211.00107","title":"Where to start? This challenges the common intuition that source-target task alignment is the main driver.","importance":"This is highly relevant to the pretrain-to-posttrain interface because it informs checkpoint selection, continual fine-tuning, and mid-training pipelines. The practical message is important: choosing a good intermediate state may be less about finding the closest source task and more about identifying target tasks that are receptive to transfer and intermediate models that are broadly strong starting points.","technical_approach":"The work is a systematic study of intertraining, not a new architecture. It compares vanilla pretrained models against models already fine-tuned on source datasets, then uses them as starting points for target fine-tuning across a range of English classification tasks. The key analytical choice is to decompose intertraining gain into a target-side component and a starting-model-side component, rather than treating each source-target pair as an indivisible unit. This is useful because it avoids over-attributing gains to semantic alignment alone. The trade-off is that the paper offers a strong empirical regularity more than a full mechanistic explanation.","experiments":"Two points are explicit in the abstract. First, the study is broad and systematic across English classification tasks. The paper tackles both in latent space.","importance":"A methodological exploration that replaces raw-text concatenation with VAE latent aggregation in RAG. Limited direct impact on pretraining, but a useful data point for how retrieval signals should enter a generative model.","technical_approach":"RegaVAE uses a Gaussian-mixture VAE whose latent space is trained to encode both source and future target information, so nearest-neighbor retrieval in that space is naturally aligned with what the LM is about to generate. For example, a model might predict both\"Anne Redpath passed away in Edinburgh.\"and\"Anne Redpath's life ended in London.\"In this work, we identify potential causes of inconsistency and evaluate the effectiveness of two mitigation strategies: up-scaling and augmenting the LM with a retrieval corpus. winner/loser 当监督信号，却很少拆开看究竟哪些可观测因素在驱动判断。这个问题现在值得重做，是因为 RLHF 和偏好模型越来越依赖大规模人工或 AI 偏好数据，如果偏好本身被长度、语气、格式等表层因素系统性污染，后续 reward model 和对齐过程会一起继承偏差。","importance":"它的价值主要是分析框架级，而不是提出新的预训练算法。对 pretrain/post-train 闭环的启示在于：偏好数据不是天然可靠标签，GPT-4 可以被用作可扩展的偏好因素分析器，帮助区分内容质量信号和表层捷径信号；这直接影响 reward modeling、自动偏好数据合成，以及对齐训练中常见的 length bias / verbosity bias 诊断。","technical_approach":"核心方法是用 GPT-4 充当可解释的偏好分析器，去解析人类偏好判断受哪些因素影响，而不是只训练一个黑盒 preference model。可以推断其关键设计是：把成对回答和人类选择输入 GPT-4，让模型给出影响判断的因素归因，再统计这些因素与最终偏好的一致性和强弱；与传统只做相关性回归或只训练二分类偏好模型不同，这种做法试图把'偏好来自什么'显式化。它的有效性依赖 GPT-4 对细粒度语言属性的稳定判断，但边界也很明确：如果 GPT-4 自身带有偏好风格，它可能把模型偏好误当成人类偏好。","experiments":"从给定信息看，实验结论应围绕'哪些因素最能解释人类偏好'以及'GPT-4 Its relevance to pretrain/post-train research is that preference data should be audited as a noisy training signal; using GPT-4 to decompose preference judgments can help separate genuine quality signals from shortcut features, which is directly useful for reward modeling, synthetic preference generation, and diagnosing alignment biases.","technical_approach":"The core idea is to use GPT-4 as an interpretable analyzer of preference factors rather than only training a black-box preference predictor. The likely mechanism is to feed response pairs plus human choices to GPT-4, ask it to identify influential factors, and then aggregate how consistently those factors explain the final preference. Compared with standard preference classification or simple feature correlations, this makes the source of preference more explicit. The design is useful if GPT-4 is stable at fine-grained judgment decomposition, but the limitation is equally clear: GPT-4 may project its own stylistic priors onto human preference explanations.","experiments":"Given the missing abstract, the experimental section can only be summarized cautiously. The natural baselines would include direct preference prediction, heuristic features such as response length or fluency, and manual factor analysis. The most valuable outcome, if supported, is not a single accuracy number but evidence that a small set of identifiable factors explains a large share of preference labels. Although the target task is sequence labeling, the broader relevance is that it applies diffusion to discrete structured prediction, which is closely related to newer non-autoregressive language modeling ideas in discrete spaces.","technical_approach":"The method treats the target tag sequence as a diffusion variable and learns to recover clean tags from noisy tag sequences. Compared with a standard token classifier, this explicitly models the joint structure of labels; compared with autoregressive tagging, it supports iterative parallel refinement. The likely design uses a discrete corruption process over tag labels and a conditional denoiser that takes the input text as context. Prior privacy work mostly focuses on memorization and extraction from model parameters, but retrieval-based systems expose private documents during inference, so the threat model changes materially.","importance":"This is a system-level safety contribution with clear implications for pretraining and deployment. Retrieval is often treated as a low-cost alternative to storing more knowledge in parameters, but that trade-off is incomplete if privacy risk is shifted rather than reduced. The paper is important because it forces a more careful comparison between parametric memory and external memory.","technical_approach":"The likely contribution is a structured privacy analysis of retrieval-based language models, separating the risks introduced by the retriever, the generator, and their interaction. From the title, the work probably studies attacks such as document extraction, membership inference, or prompt-based recovery of sensitive content, and asks whether leakage comes from retrieval itself or from generation amplifying retrieved text. This differs from standard memorization studies by auditing the full RLM pipeline.","experiments":"Without the abstract, exact numbers cannot be stated. The natural setup would compare LM-only and retrieval-augmented systems while varying private corpus access, top-k retrieval, deduplication, and filtering. Likely metrics include attack success rate, extraction rate, or membership inference AUC. The most important result, if supported, would be that retrieval changes the privacy surface in a nontrivial way: it may reduce some forms of parameter memorization while making direct content leakage easier in others. Earlier approaches usually concatenate the query with the document or add relevance features, but the generation objective itself does not strongly enforce query faithfulness throughout decoding.","importance":"This is mainly a task-level modeling contribution, but it is also relevant to alternative LM paradigms because it applies diffusion language modeling to conditional summarization with an explicit relevance signal. The broader lesson is that diffusion-style generation may be a useful vehicle for injecting global control signals beyond left-to-right conditioning.","technical_approach":"The method is a diffusion language model for query-focused summarization, augmented with query-document relevance. Compared with standard autoregressive summarizers, it produces the summary via iterative denoising; compared with plain diffusion text generation, it explicitly injects relevance between the query and source document as a conditioning or guidance signal. This design is reasonable because query relevance is a global constraint, and simple input concatenation may not be enough to prevent topical drift in long summaries.","experiments":"The missing abstract means the exact datasets and ROUGE gains cannot be reported. The main lesson is that multilingual capability is not only about token coverage or data proportion; it is also about decoupling the language of reasoning from the language of output. That makes it relevant to how we interpret internal multilingual representations in LLMs.","technical_approach":"The core idea is cross-lingual-thought prompting: let the model perform intermediate reasoning in a stronger language, then produce the answer in the target language. Compared with native target-language chain-of-thought, this explicitly exploits language asymmetry; compared with translate-reason-translate pipelines, it keeps the process inside one model instead of relying on a separate MT system. Bean","Bertie Vidgen","Paul Röttger","Scott Hale"],"institutions":["Oxford Internet Institute","Alan Turing Institute"],"abstract":"Human feedback is increasingly used to steer the behaviours of Large Language Models (LLMs). However, it is unclear how to collect and incorporate feedback in a way that is efficient, effective and unbiased, especially for highly subjective human preferences and values. In this paper, we survey existing approaches for learning from human feedback, drawing on 95 papers primarily from the ACL and arXiv repositories.First, we summarise the past, pre-LLM trends for integrating human feedback into language models. Second, we give an overview of present techniques and practices, as well as the motivations for using feedback; conceptual frameworks for defining values and preferences; and how feedback is collected and from whom. Finally, we encourage a better future of feedback learning in LLMs by raising five unresolved conceptual and practical challenges.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.07629","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/KirkBVRH23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.148"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/KirkBVRH23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.148","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"RLHF 被广泛使用，但在处理主观人类偏好时，反馈的收集和整合方式缺乏标准，容易引入偏见且效率低下。","importance":"范式级综述。系统梳理了 pre-LLM 到当前 RLHF 的演进，指出了对齐数据采样的核心痛点（谁的偏好、如何定义价值观），对 post-train 数据构建有直接指导意义。","technical_approach":"综述性工作。分析了 95 篇文献，解构了反馈学习的动机、价值观定义框架以及反馈收集机制，呼吁建立更透明、更多元的人类反馈收集范式。","experiments":"综述文章，无具体模型实验。核心贡献是提出了反馈学习的分类法和未来研究框架。","authors_note":"Hannah Kirk (Oxford), Bertie Vidgen"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"While RLHF is widely used, the methods for collecting and incorporating feedback—especially for subjective human preferences—lack standardization, leading to bias and inefficiency.","importance":"Paradigm-level survey. For pretraining-style research, this matters because it suggests information flow in multi-model training need not be strictly top-down; smaller models may contribute useful inductive bias or stabilization back to larger ones.","technical_approach":"The method, Co-Training and Co-Distillation (CTCD), jointly trains a large and a small model while distilling knowledge in both directions. Instead of treating the issue as purely missing logical rules, the authors argue that pragmatic reasoning is the missing ingredient.","importance":"This is primarily a semantics/reasoning analysis paper, but it has a useful pretraining implication: large-scale next-token training does not automatically produce robust compositional meaning, especially when interpretation is pragmatically constrained.
