Steedman"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$38","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.14552","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/McKennaLCH0S23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.FINDINGS-EMNLP.182"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/McKennaLCH0S23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.findings-emnlp.182","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的问题是：LLM 在推理任务上产生“幻觉”时，错因到底来自推理能力不足，还是来自预训练学到的偏置。此前很多工作把 NLI、QA、摘要中的错误笼统归因于 reasoning failure；作者用受控行为实验指出，至少在一部分场景下，模型错误可以被两个更基础的预训练来源解释：句子级记忆和词项共现模式。","importance":"这项工作的贡献是机制分析层面的，而且对 pretrain 很相关。它的重要性在于把 hallucination 的一部分责任从“推理模块不行”转回“预训练统计偏置”，这会影响我们如何设计数据去重、数据配比、评测以及 post-training 对齐。如果错误主要由记忆触发和 lexical prior 驱动，那么单纯堆 chain-of-thought 或 instruction tuning 不能从根上解决。","technical_approach":"核心方法是行为探针而不是新模型。作者在多类 LLM（LLaMA、GPT-3.5、PaLM）上构造控制实验，分别测试：当 hypothesis 在训练数据中被见过时，模型是否会无视 premise 倾向于判 entailment；以及当某些词对在预训练里高频共现时，模型是否会把这种统计相关误当语义蕴含。关键设计是把 premise-hypothesis 关系与训练语料可记忆性、词项使用模式解耦，这样能更直接定位错误来源。和普通 benchmark 相关性分析不同，它试图给出可操作的因果候选机制。","experiments":"实验结论很明确：多个 LLM 家族都显示出两类偏置，而且这两类偏置能解释 NLI 等推理任务中的大量错误。第一，若 hypothesis 是训练中可检索到的句子，模型更容易输出 entailment，即使 premise 不支持；第二，实体会像索引一样触发记忆片段，词汇层面的使用统计也会影响判断。论文强调这些现象在受控设置下跨模型出现，因此不是单一架构偶然失效。边界是，文章更像定性+行为证据而非完整量化归因：它证明这些偏置“很重要”，但未必穷尽全部 hallucination 来源。","authors_note":"机构与代表性团队信息无法从给定材料中可靠确认，返回空字符串。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper asks whether hallucinations on inference tasks are really failures of reasoning, or whether they are often driven by biases inherited from pretraining. Much prior discussion loosely attributes NLI-style errors to poor reasoning; this paper argues that a substantial part of the behavior can instead be explained by memorized sentences and lexical co-occurrence priors.","importance":"This is a mechanism-analysis contribution with clear pretraining relevance. Its significance is that it shifts some blame from downstream reasoning failure back to pretraining statistics, which matters for deduplication, data design, evaluation, and alignment. If the model is being steered by memory triggers and lexical priors, then post-hoc prompting alone will not fix the root cause.","technical_approach":"The method is a set of controlled behavioral probes rather than a new model. Across LLaMA, GPT-3.5, and PaLM, the authors test whether models over-predict entailment when the hypothesis is attested in training data, and whether token-level usage patterns are mistaken for semantic entailment. The key experimental choice is to separate premise-hypothesis semantics from memorization and lexical statistics, so the error source can be isolated more cleanly than with standard benchmark correlation studies.","experiments":"The central empirical finding is that both biases appear across multiple LLM families and explain a substantial fraction of failures on inference tasks. First, if a hypothesis is memorized or appears familiar from training, models tend to label it as entailed even when the premise does not support it. Second, entities can act as indices into memorized text, and broad lexical usage statistics also distort judgments. The strength of the paper is the cross-model consistency under controlled settings. The limitation is that the work offers strong behavioral evidence for important sources of hallucination, but not a complete quantitative decomposition of all error sources.","authors_note":""},"tags":["hallucination","nli","pretraining-bias","llm-behavior"],"notable_authors":["Nick McKenna","Tianyi Li","Liang Cheng","Mohammad Javad Hosseini","Mark Johnson"]},{"id":"MinixhoferPV23","title":"CompoundPiece: Evaluating and Improving Decompounding Performance of Language Models","authors":["Benjamin Minixhofer","Jonas Pfeiffer","Ivan Vulic"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/MinixhoferPV23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.24"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/MinixhoferPV23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.24","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的问题是：语言模型在处理复合词，尤其是需要 decompounding 的语言现象时，表现到底有多差，以及如何系统改善。复合词会把词法、分词和语义组合问题捆在一起；如果 tokenizer 和预训练没有学好这种组合规律，模型在生成、理解和迁移上都可能吃亏。","importance":"这项工作对 pretrain 的意义主要落在 tokenizer 与形态泛化分析上。它的重要性在于把一个常被更大规模平均掉的细节单独拎出来：对德语等富复合构词语言，subword 方案未必自然解决 decompounding，错误的切分会直接影响词义组合和参数共享。","technical_approach":"从题目可确认，这篇论文同时做了两件事：先评估语言模型的 decompounding 能力，再提出改进方法。合理推测其方法会围绕复合词分解数据、评测基准或 tokenizer/训练改造展开，但给定材料没有摘要，无法可靠写出具体算法、训练目标或与 prior 的精确差异。","experiments":"由于缺少摘要和实验描述，无法安全给出模型规模、数据集和具体数字。可以确定的是，论文关注“评估 + 改进”两部分，并试图把 decompounding 从一般词汇泛化问题中分离出来单独测量；但具体结论需要原文支持，不能编造。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper studies how well language models handle decompounding, and how to improve that ability. Prior work often tries to patch this at probing or fine-tuning time; this paper instead modifies pretraining representations directly.","importance":"This is a genuine pretraining representation-learning contribution. Its importance is methodological: rather than assuming next-token prediction will implicitly learn all lexical structure, it treats lexical semantic relations as first-class training signals and uses them to shape hidden states throughout pretraining.","technical_approach":"The core method is BALAUR, which models lexical semantic relations directly in the hidden states of the LM during pretraining. The key hypothesis is that hidden representations form a controllable interface, so constraining them can change downstream linguistic behavior. Concretely, the method augments standard LM training with relation-aware structure in representation space. Compared with approaches that add lexical supervision only at the output layer or during downstream fine-tuning, BALAUR intervenes throughout pretraining, aiming to make lexical reasoning part of the model’s internal geometry rather than an attached classifier behavior.","experiments":"From the abstract, the paper reports that BALAUR validates the central hypothesis and improves over baselines on lexical inference tasks, especially those involving hypernymy. The abstract does not provide model sizes or exact gains, so the safe summary is qualitative. The more interesting point is not one benchmark number but the broader implication: targeted representational shaping during pretraining can repair persistent linguistic weaknesses that generic LM objectives leave unresolved. The open question is how far such explicit supervision scales and whether it trades off against broader open-domain language modeling ability.","authors_note":"McGill University. Jackie C. K. Lelkes","Honglei Zhuang","Jimmy Lin","Donald Metzler","Vinh Q. Tran"],"institutions":["University of Waterloo","Google Research"],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/Pradeep0GLZLM023","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.83"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/Pradeep0GLZLM023","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.83","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"生成式检索（generative retrieval）用 seq2seq 模型直接生成文档标识符来完成检索，在小规模语料上表现不错，但能否 scale 到百万级文档一直是未解问题。本文系统评估了这一 scaling 瓶颈。","importance":"对 pretrain 研究的启示在于：生成式检索本质上是用语言模型做记忆和检索，其 scaling 行为揭示了 LM 参数化记忆的容量边界。结论偏负面——生成式检索在大规模下显著退化，这对'用 LM 替代传统索引'的范式是重要的现实检验。","technical_approach":"在 NQ 等标准检索数据集上，将语料规模从几千逐步扩展到百万级，评估 DSI（Differentiable Search Index）及其变体的检索质量变化。对比 baseline 包括 BM25 和 dual-encoder 等传统/神经检索方法。核心发现是生成式检索的性能随语料规模增长而显著下降，而传统方法相对稳健。","experiments":"在 NQ320K 到 MS MARCO 8.8M 规模上测试。生成式检索在小规模（如 NQ320K）上有竞争力，但扩展到百万级后 Recall 大幅下降，与 dual-encoder 的差距拉大。具体数字需参考原文表格，但趋势明确：scaling 是生成式检索的硬伤。","authors_note":"Ronak Pradeep 来自 University of Waterloo（Jimmy Lin 组），该组是信息检索领域的顶级团队。Honglei Zhuang、Donald Metzler 等来自 Google Research。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Generative retrieval uses seq2seq models to directly generate document identifiers for retrieval. It works well on small corpora, but whether it scales to millions of passages has been an open question. This paper provides a systematic evaluation of that scaling bottleneck.","importance":"The finding is largely negative: generative retrieval degrades significantly at scale, while traditional and dual-encoder methods remain robust. For pretrain researchers, this reveals capacity limits of parametric memory in LMs—you cannot simply scale up a generative model to replace an index over millions of documents.","technical_approach":"Systematically scales corpus size from thousands to millions of passages on NQ and MS MARCO, evaluating DSI variants against BM25 and dual-encoder baselines. ReCEval proposes evaluating each reasoning step along two dimensions: correctness and informativeness.","importance":"A method-level contribution to reasoning evaluation. For pretrain research, it suggests that finer-grained evaluation signals—beyond final-answer EM/F1—are needed to assess and improve reasoning capabilities injected during pretraining or mid-training. Relevant to reward model design and data filtering.","technical_approach":"Decomposes reasoning chain evaluation into step-level assessment. Correctness checks logical consistency and factual accuracy of each step; informativeness checks whether each step contributes necessary new information toward the conclusion. Can be automated via NLI models or LLMs.","experiments":"Tested on GSM8K, EntailmentBank, and similar reasoning datasets. Automatic ReCEval scores correlate well with human judgments of reasoning chain quality. The key finding is that step-level evaluation discriminates chain quality better than final-answer-only metrics.","authors_note":"From UNC Chapel Hill (Mohit Bansal's group), prolific in NLG evaluation, reasoning, and multimodal research."},"tags":["reasoning","cot","evaluation"],"notable_authors":["Mohit Bansal"]},{"id":"PratoHPSC23","title":"EpiK-Eval: Evaluation for Language Models as Epistemic Models","authors":["Gabriele Prato","Jerry Huang","Prasanna Parthasarathi","Shagun Sodhani","Sarath Chandar"],"institutions":["Mila","Polytechnique Montréal"],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/PratoHPSC23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.593"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/PratoHPSC23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.593","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"LLM 作为知识库使用时，其内部知识的一致性（epistemic consistency）缺乏系统评估。EpiK-Eval 构建了一个评估框架，测试模型是否能在不同问法下对同一事实保持一致的回答。","importance":"评估级贡献。对 pretrain 研究的启示：模型在预训练中学到的知识如果在不同 prompt 下表现不一致，说明知识的编码是脆弱的。这为评估 pretrain 数据质量和知识注入效果提供了一个新角度。","technical_approach":"构建了一组需要多步推理才能回答的事实性问题，每个问题有多种等价的问法（paraphrase）。评估模型在这些等价问法下的回答一致性。核心指标是跨 paraphrase 的答案一致率，而非单纯的准确率。","experiments":"在 GPT-3.5、GPT-4 等模型上测试，发现即使是最强的模型也存在显著的 epistemic inconsistency——同一事实换个问法就可能给出矛盾答案。具体不一致率需查原文。","authors_note":"来自 Mila / Polytechnique Montréal，Sarath Chandar 组在 continual learning 和 LLM 评估方面有持续产出。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"When LLMs are used as knowledge bases, the consistency of their internal knowledge across different phrasings of the same question is poorly understood. EpiK-Eval provides a benchmark to measure this epistemic consistency.","importance":"An evaluation-level contribution. For pretrain researchers, inconsistency across paraphrases signals fragile knowledge encoding during pretraining. This benchmark offers a new lens for assessing data quality and knowledge retention in pretrained models.","technical_approach":"Constructs factual questions requiring multi-hop reasoning, each with multiple equivalent phrasings. Measures cross-paraphrase answer consistency rather than just accuracy. The key metric is how often the model gives the same answer to semantically equivalent queries.","experiments":"Tested on GPT-3.5, GPT-4, and others. Even the strongest models show significant epistemic inconsistency—contradicting themselves when the same fact is asked differently. Specific inconsistency rates are in the paper.","authors_note":"From Mila / Polytechnique Montréal. Sarath Chandar's group works on continual learning and LLM evaluation."},"tags":["knowledge","evaluation","hallucination"],"notable_authors":["Sarath Chandar"]},{"id":"PryzantI0L0023","title":"Automatic Prompt Optimization with &quot;Gradient Descent&quot; and Beam Search","authors":["Reid Pryzant","Dan Iter","Jerry Li","Yin Tat Lee","Chenguang Zhu","Michael Zeng"],"institutions":["Microsoft Research"],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/PryzantI0L0023","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.494"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/PryzantI0L0023","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.494","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"手动编写 LLM prompt 费时且不稳定，自动 prompt 优化方法大多需要梯度或大量标注数据。本文提出用自然语言'梯度'（文本反馈）和 beam search 来自动优化 prompt，无需访问模型参数。","importance":"方法级贡献。对 pretrain 研究的间接价值：自动 prompt 优化揭示了 LLM 对指令措辞的敏感性，这反映了预训练阶段指令理解能力的编码方式。此外，该方法可用于高效筛选 pretrain/SFT 数据的 prompt 模板。","technical_approach":"核心思路是把 prompt 优化类比为梯度下降：用一个 LLM 生成当前 prompt 的'文本梯度'（即对 prompt 的自然语言改进建议），然后用另一个 LLM 根据这些建议生成新的 prompt 候选，再用 beam search 在候选中选择最优。整个过程是黑盒的，不需要模型梯度。关键设计是将 mini-batch 的错误样本作为反馈信号。","experiments":"在 BBH（BIG-Bench Hard）、GSM8K 等任务上测试，与人工 prompt 和 APE（Automatic Prompt Engineer）对比。在多数任务上超过人工 prompt，在部分任务上超过 APE。提升幅度因任务而异，通常在 2-10 个百分点。","authors_note":"Reid Pryzant 来自 Microsoft Research，Yin Tat Lee 是优化理论领域的知名学者。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Manual prompt engineering is time-consuming and brittle. Most automatic prompt optimization methods require gradients or extensive labeled data. This paper proposes optimizing prompts using natural language 'gradients' (textual feedback) and beam search, without access to model parameters.","importance":"A method-level contribution. For pretrain researchers, the sensitivity of LLMs to prompt wording—which this method exploits—reflects how instruction-following is encoded during pretraining. The approach can also be used to efficiently search prompt templates for data curation.","technical_approach":"Analogizes prompt optimization to gradient descent: one LLM generates 'text gradients' (natural language suggestions for improving the prompt based on error analysis), another LLM produces new prompt candidates from these suggestions, and beam search selects the best. Fully black-box—no model gradients needed. Key design: uses mini-batch error samples as feedback signal.","experiments":"Tested on BBH, GSM8K, and other tasks against human-written prompts and APE. Outperforms human prompts on most tasks and APE on several, with improvements typically in the 2-10 percentage point range depending on the task.","authors_note":"Reid Pryzant from Microsoft Research. Yin Tat Lee is a well-known optimization theorist."},"tags":["prompt-optimization","apo"],"notable_authors":["Yin Tat Lee"]},{"id":"PurkayasthaRPGV23","title":"Romanization-based Large-scale Adaptation of Multilingual Language Models","authors":["Sukannya Purkayastha","Sebastian Ruder","Jonas Pfeiffer","Iryna Gurevych","Ivan Vulic"],"institutions":["Google DeepMind","TU Darmstadt","University of Cambridge"],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/PurkayasthaRPGV23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.FINDINGS-EMNLP.538"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/PurkayasthaRPGV23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.findings-emnlp.538","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"多语言 LM 对低资源语言（尤其是使用非拉丁文字的语言）覆盖不足，因为 tokenizer 和预训练数据都偏向高资源语言。本文探索用罗马化（romanization，即把非拉丁文字转写为拉丁字母）来弥合这一差距。","importance":"方法级贡献，对多语言预训练有直接启示。罗马化是一种低成本的输入规范化手段，可以让低资源语言复用高资源拉丁语系语言的 tokenizer 和表示空间。这为多语言 pretrain 的 tokenizer 设计和数据处理提供了一个实用思路。","technical_approach":"将低资源语言的文本通过标准罗马化方案（如 Urdu→拉丁转写）转换后输入多语言 LM（如 mBERT、XLM-R），然后用 adapter 进行适配。核心假设是罗马化后的文本与英语等高资源语言在 token 空间上更接近，从而能更好地利用预训练知识。对比了直接用原始文字 vs 罗马化后的效果。","experiments":"在多种低资源语言（如乌尔都语、印地语、阿拉伯语等）的 NER、分类等下游任务上测试。罗马化 + adapter 在多数低资源语言上优于直接使用原始文字，尤其在 tokenizer 对该语言覆盖差的情况下提升更明显。但对已有良好 tokenizer 覆盖的语言，罗马化的收益有限。","authors_note":"Sebastian Ruder（Google DeepMind）是多语言 NLP 领域的标志性人物。Jonas Pfeiffer 和 Iryna Gurevych 来自 TU Darmstadt，Ivan Vulić 来自 Cambridge。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Multilingual LMs underserve low-resource languages, especially those with non-Latin scripts, due to tokenizer and data biases. This paper explores romanization—transliterating non-Latin text to Latin script—as a way to bridge this gap.","importance":"A method-level contribution to multilingual pretraining. Romanization is a low-cost input normalization that lets low-resource languages reuse the tokenizer and representation space of high-resource Latin-script languages. Directly relevant to tokenizer design and data processing for multilingual pretrain.","technical_approach":"Converts low-resource language text to Latin script via standard romanization schemes, then feeds it into multilingual LMs (mBERT, XLM-R) with adapters for task adaptation. The hypothesis is that romanized text is closer to English in token space, enabling better transfer. Compares original script vs. romanized input systematically.","experiments":"Tested on NER, classification, and other tasks across Urdu, Hindi, Arabic, and other low-resource languages. Romanization + adapters outperform original-script input in most low-resource settings, especially when the tokenizer has poor coverage of the target script. Gains diminish for languages already well-covered by the tokenizer.","authors_note":"Sebastian Ruder (Google DeepMind) is a leading figure in multilingual NLP. Jonas Pfeiffer and Iryna Gurevych from TU Darmstadt; Ivan Vulić from Cambridge."},"tags":["multilingual","tokenizer","romanization"],"notable_authors":["Sebastian Ruder","Jonas Pfeiffer","Iryna Gurevych","Ivan Vulić"]},{"id":"QiFB23","title":"Cross-Lingual Consistency of Factual Knowledge in Multilingual Language Models","authors":["Jirui Qi","Raquel Fernández","Arianna Bisazza"],"institutions":["University of Groningen"],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/QiFB23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.658"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/QiFB23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.658","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"多语言 LM 在不同语言中对同一事实的回答是否一致？已有工作主要关注单语言的事实准确性，跨语言一致性（cross-lingual factual consistency）缺乏系统研究。","importance":"评估级贡献。对多语言预训练的直接启示：如果模型在英语中知道某个事实但在其他语言中回答错误或矛盾，说明预训练中的知识并未真正跨语言对齐。这为评估多语言 pretrain 数据配比和对齐策略提供了新维度。","technical_approach":"构建跨语言事实一致性评估框架：用多语言知识探测（knowledge probing）在多种语言中查询同一事实，比较模型在不同语言下的回答是否一致。分析了语言相似度、训练数据量等因素对一致性的影响。","experiments":"在 mBERT 和 XLM-R 上测试多种语言对。发现跨语言事实一致性远低于预期——模型在高资源语言（如英语）中正确的事实，在低资源语言中经常给出不同甚至矛盾的答案。语言间的一致性与训练数据量和语言相似度正相关。","authors_note":"来自 University of Groningen，Arianna Bisazza 在多语言 NLP 方面有持续产出。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Do multilingual LMs give consistent answers to the same factual question across languages? Prior work focuses on monolingual factual accuracy; cross-lingual factual consistency has been understudied.","importance":"An evaluation-level contribution. For multilingual pretraining, inconsistency across languages means knowledge learned in one language is not truly aligned across languages. Bennett"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/RossetZD0B23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.702"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/RossetZD0B23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.702","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是长篇问答中的偏好建模不稳定：同样答案可能在事实性、覆盖度、结构、引用方式等多个维度上好坏不同，简单的成对偏好学习往往把这些标准混在一起。过去 RLHF 或 ranker 常直接从总体偏好标签学一个黑盒分数，但在 long-form QA 里这种做法很难解释，也难以控制评估准则。","importance":"这是偏 post-train 接口但对训练信号质量很有启发的工作。它的重要性在于把‘偏好’从单一分数拆成公理化的可解释约束，说明长答案质量建模需要更细的结构化信号。对 pretrain/posttrain 闭环的价值是：如果偏好目标本身定义得更稳，后续 SFT/RL 的优化方向也会更少受标注噪声和奖励黑箱影响。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 axiomatic preference modeling：不直接把偏好视为不可解释的 latent score，而是用一组显式公理或规则来约束长答案比较。典型做法可能是把答案质量分解为若干可判定维度，再通过满足这些公理的一致性学习偏好模型。它和普通 reward model 的区别在于，训练目标不仅追求拟合偏好标签，还追求符合预先指定的比较原则；这有助于减少标签稀疏时的任意性。代价是公理设计会带来先验偏置，如果定义得不对，模型会系统性偏好某种文风或结构而非真实质量。","experiments":"从题目判断，实验应集中在 long-form QA 偏好预测、reranking 或与人偏好一致性评估上，并与标准 pairwise preference model 或 pointwise ranker 比较。当前没有摘要，无法确认使用的数据集、标注规模、主要指标以及提升幅度。若论文做得扎实，最值得看的实验会是跨数据集泛化、对不同质量维度的控制性分析，以及公理移除后的性能退化，用来验证结构化偏好约束是否真的优于黑盒拟合。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets a real weakness in long-form QA: preference labels collapse multiple quality dimensions into a single signal, making learned reward or ranking models unstable and hard to interpret. Standard pairwise preference learning often works as a black box, but that is a poor fit when answers differ along factuality, coverage, organization, and citation quality at the same time.","importance":"This is more of a post-training and evaluation paper, but it matters for training-signal quality. Its contribution is to make preference modeling more structured and explicit, which is valuable wherever reward models are later used for reranking, SFT filtering, or RL. The broader lesson is that better-defined preferences can reduce noise and hidden bias in the optimization target.","technical_approach":"The method uses axiomatic preference modeling: instead of learning a single opaque score from pairwise labels, it imposes explicit principles on how long-form answers should be compared. The likely benefit is better consistency and interpretability, especially when labels are sparse or heterogeneous. Compared with a standard reward model, the objective is not only to fit preferences but also to satisfy predefined comparison rules. The trade-off is that the axioms inject prior assumptions; if they are poorly chosen, the model may overvalue style or structure rather than genuine answer quality.","experiments":"The expected evaluation setting is long-form QA preference prediction, answer reranking, or agreement with human judgments, compared against standard pairwise or pointwise preference models. Prior discussion around outliers often centered on quantization and stability, treating them as nuisances rather than functional carriers of information.","importance":"This is an interpretability and representation-analysis contribution with practical implications. If task-relevant information is concentrated in a small set of outlier dimensions, then assumptions of uniformly distributed knowledge become less reliable for pruning, quantization, adapter design, and activation interventions.","technical_approach":"The likely method is to identify outlier dimensions in hidden representations and test whether they matter disproportionately for downstream tasks. A convincing setup would compare these dimensions against random or non-outlier controls through masking, ablation, or perturbation, and then measure task degradation. The key design choice is to move beyond correlation: the claim only matters if intervention on outlier dimensions changes behavior much more than intervention on matched controls. The main boundary is generality—such concentration may depend on layer, task, or fine-tuning regime.","experiments":"The expected core finding is that ablating a small number of outlier dimensions hurts task performance far more than ablating random dimensions, supporting the idea that they carry task-specific knowledge. Useful baselines would include random-dimension ablation, non-outlier high-variance dimensions, and layer-wise comparisons. Without the abstract and tables, I cannot state the exact models, tasks, or effect sizes. Strong evidence would include replication across tasks and differences between pretrained and fine-tuned states.","authors_note":""},"tags":["outlier-dimensions","representations","interpretability"]},{"id":"Ryu23","title":"Plausibility Processing in Transformer Language Models: Focusing on the Role of Attention Heads in GPT","authors":["Soo Ryu"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/Ryu23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.FINDINGS-EMNLP.27"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/Ryu23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.findings-emnlp.27","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是 Transformer 语言模型如何处理 plausibility，也就是句子是否符合常识或语义合理性，尤其想定位到 GPT 中具体由哪些注意力头承担这类处理。过去很多工作能证明模型整体区分合理/不合理句子，但较少把责任进一步拆到 head 级机制上。","importance":"这是细粒度机制分析工作，贡献量级偏认知与可解释性，而非直接改进训练算法。对 pretrain 的启示在于：如果 plausibility processing 明显集中在少量头或特定层，那么训练信号、知识组织和可干预性可能比我们想的更局部，这会影响后续对 head pruning、功能模块化和安全干预的判断。","technical_approach":"核心方法应是围绕 plausibility 设计刺激材料，再分析 GPT 各注意力头的激活、贡献或干预效应。常见路径包括 attention pattern inspection、head ablation、对数概率变化分析，以及比较合理与不合理输入时头级响应差异。关键不是观察到某些头‘看起来更活跃’，而是证明干预这些头会系统性改变模型对 plausibility 的判断。它和一般 attention 可视化的区别在于有明确语言现象和可检验假设。边界在于 attention 头功能常随上下文而变，单头因果解释容易被 residual stream 和 MLP 路径稀释。","experiments":"从题目看，实验应围绕 GPT 类模型上的 plausibility 现象展开，核心结果很可能是识别出少量对合理性判断贡献显著的头，且这些头在某些层集中出现。当前无摘要，无法确认具体模型版本、刺激集大小、显著性检验和效应量。若论文可信，最值得看的证据是：随机头消融对 plausibility 影响很小，而目标头消融影响显著；以及这种现象在不同类型的常识/语义违例上是否一致。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper investigates how Transformer language models process plausibility, with a specific focus on whether particular attention heads in GPT are responsible for that behavior. Prior work often showed that models are sensitive to plausible versus implausible text at the model level, but stopped short of identifying fine-grained circuit components.","importance":"This is a mechanistic interpretability paper rather than a training-method paper. Its value lies in testing whether a semantic function like plausibility is localized enough to be mapped to specific heads or layers. If that localization is real, it affects how we think about pruning, intervention, and where certain kinds of knowledge live inside pretrained models.","technical_approach":"The likely approach is to construct plausibility-sensitive stimuli and analyze head-level behavior in GPT through attention inspection, head ablation, and probability shifts. The design matters because simple attention visualization is not enough; the stronger claim requires showing that manipulating certain heads systematically changes plausibility-related behavior. The method is therefore closer to targeted causal probing than descriptive analysis. The main caveat is that head functions are often context-dependent and can be partially bypassed by residual and MLP pathways.","experiments":"The key empirical result is likely the identification of a small subset of heads whose ablation disproportionately affects plausibility judgments. Static width reduction saves compute, but it imposes the same smaller model on easy and hard cases alike.","importance":"This is an architecture and systems paper on conditional computation. Its relevance to pretraining is that it explores dynamic capacity allocation along the width dimension, which is a useful alternative to expert routing or static compression. If stable, this idea could influence future training and serving designs where not all layers or inputs need the same active width.","technical_approach":"SHARCS uses routing over dynamic-width subnetworks inside Transformers. The key idea is to activate different width configurations depending on the input, instead of committing to one fixed reduced model. This differs from standard pruning because the model remains elastic at runtime. The value is obvious: save compute on easier inputs while preserving capacity for harder ones. The difficult part is training the router and preventing collapse or interference across subnetworks.","experiments":"The likely evaluation compares SHARCS against standard Transformers, static-width baselines, and other adaptive-compute methods under matched quality targets. Addresses forgetting from a geometric perspective of the parameter space, providing a strict mathematically constrained method for continual pre-training and lossless knowledge updating in LLMs.","technical_approach":"Employs orthogonal subspace learning. When learning a new task, it computes the SVD of old task features to find the parameter subspace relied upon by old tasks. The gradients of the new task are then projected onto the orthogonal complement of this subspace for updating, mathematically ensuring that new updates do not alter the outputs of old tasks.","experiments":"Under continual learning settings across a sequence of NLP tasks, this method significantly reduces the catastrophic forgetting rate, outperforming traditional regularization In some attention heads, the attention focuses on the neighbors of each token. This allows the output vector of each token to depend on the surrounding tokens and contributes to make the inference context-dependent. We analyze the mechanism behind the concentration of attention on nearby tokens. We show that the phenomenon emerges as follows: (1) learned position embedding has sinusoid-like components, (2) such components are transmitted to the query and the key in the self-attention, (3) the attention head shifts the phases of the sinusoid-like components so that the attention concentrates on nearby tokens at speciﬁc relative positions. In other words, a certain type of Transformer-based model acquires the sinusoidal positional encoding to some extent on its own through Masked Language Modeling.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/YamamotoM23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.2"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/YamamotoM23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.2","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"Transformer 中某些 attention head 会集中关注邻近 token，形成局部注意力模式。这种现象的内在机制是什么？具体来说，学习到的绝对位置编码 (APE) 是如何实现基于相对位置的注意力的？","importance":"机制分析级贡献。对 pretrain 研究有直接启发：揭示了学习型 APE 会自发习得类正弦波成分，并通过 Q/K 投影的相位偏移实现相对位置注意力。这解释了为什么 APE 在实践中能部分替代显式相对位置编码（如 RoPE），也为理解位置编码的设计空间提供了机制层面的证据。","technical_approach":"分析路径分三步：(1) 发现学习到的 APE 包含类正弦波成分；(2) 这些成分通过 self-attention 的 Q/K 线性变换被传递；(3) attention head 通过调整正弦波的相位，使注意力集中在特定相对位置上。这实际上说明 APE 隐式地学到了相对位置编码的功能。分析方法是对学习到的 embedding 做频谱分析，再追踪信号在 Q/K 投影中的传播。","experiments":"在 Transformer 模型上分析学习到的位置编码，验证了正弦波成分的存在及其在 Q/K 中的传播和相位偏移机制。具体模型规模和任务未在摘要中说明。这是一项分析性工作，核心价值在于机制解释而非性能提升。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Some attention heads in Transformers concentrate on nearby tokens, creating local attention patterns. The question is: how does learned absolute position embedding (APE) achieve relative-position-based attention?","importance":"Mechanistic analysis contribution. Directly relevant to pretrain research: it reveals that learned APE spontaneously acquires sinusoid-like components, and Q/K projections shift their phases to create relative position attention. This explains why APE can partially substitute explicit relative position encodings (e.g., RoPE) and informs the design space of position encodings.","technical_approach":"Three-step analysis: (1) learned APE contains sinusoid-like frequency components; (2) these components propagate through Q/K linear projections in self-attention; (3) attention heads shift the phases of these sinusoidal components to concentrate attention at specific relative positions. The analysis uses spectral decomposition of learned embeddings and traces signal propagation through Q/K projections.","experiments":"Analyzes learned position embeddings in Transformer models, confirming the existence of sinusoidal components and their phase-shift mechanism in Q/K. Specific model scale and tasks not stated in the abstract. This is an analytical work; its value is in mechanistic explanation rather than performance gains.","authors_note":""},"tags":["position-embeddings","interpretability","attention-mechanism"]},{"id":"2310.14709","title":"Once Upon a Time in Graph: Relative-Time Pretraining for Complex Temporal Reasoning","authors":["Sen Yang","Xin Li","Lidong Bing","Wai Lam"],"institutions":["Alibaba DAMO Academy","Chinese University of Hong Kong"],"abstract":"$42","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.14709","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/YangLBL23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.728"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/YangLBL23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.728","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"预训练语言模型难以理解和推理文本中的时间上下文，现有方法侧重于将文本与时间戳直接关联，但这种关联不足以支撑需要跨知识时间依赖推理的下游任务。","importance":"方法级贡献。对 pretrain 研究的启示在于：将时间关系建模为图结构并融入预训练，是一种将结构化先验注入语言模型的思路。但适用范围较窄，主要面向时间推理相关任务。","technical_approach":"提出 RemeMo (Relative Time Modeling)：利用事件在一维时间轴上的相对位置关系构建图结构，然后基于这个图进行预训练。核心思路是把时间推理从「文本-时间戳」的直接关联转变为「事件-事件」的相对时间关系建模。与直接在文本中注入时间标记的方法相比，图结构能更自然地表达传递性等时间推理所需的关系。","experiments":"摘要中未给出具体实验数据。核心声称是 RemeMo 在需要复杂时间推理的下游任务上优于仅做文本-时间戳关联的方法。","authors_note":"Sen Yang, Lidong Bing（阿里达摩院 NLP 团队）。Lidong Bing 在多语言 NLP 和信息抽取方向有广泛工作。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Pretrained LMs struggle with temporal reasoning. The goal is to find a single representative subset that effectively prompts diverse test instances within a task.","importance":"Method-level contribution. Indirect pretrain relevance: the demonstration selection problem reflects how models utilize context information—what context signals help most, paralleling data selection logic in pretraining.","technical_approach":"Two-stage Determinantal Point Process (DPP): stage 1 ensures individual quality of each demonstration, stage 2 ensures diversity across the subset. DPP naturally favors diverse selections. Unlike per-instance selection methods, this produces a fixed subset for the entire task, greatly reducing inference-time selection overhead.","experiments":"Specific numbers not provided in the abstract. Core finding: demonstration subsets meeting both high quality and high diversity criteria improve model performance, and two-stage DPP effectively achieves this.","authors_note":"Jun Zhao, Kang Liu (Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences), long-standing work in information extraction and knowledge graphs."},"tags":["icl","demonstration-selection","dpp","few-shot"],"notable_authors":["Jun Zhao","Kang Liu"]},{"id":"2310.05857","title":"Improving Summarization with Human Edits","authors":["Zonghai Yao","Benjamin J. Confidence calibration, an effective analysis method for gauging the reliability of deep models, serves as a crucial tool for assessing and improving their reliability. However, such investigation has been comparatively underexplored. In this work, we conduct a systematic examination of the calibration of aligned language models throughout the entire construction process, including pretraining and alignment training. At each stage, we investigate how different training settings, such as parameter scales and training data, affect model calibration. To thoroughly assess model calibration, we evaluate models on three most concerned aspects: generation, factuality and understanding. Heterogeneous Graphs, TAHGs）中实体间的拓扑关系和异构类型信息。学术网络、社交平台等场景中，实体既有文本又有多种关系，单独建模文本会丢失结构信号。","importance":"方法级贡献，提出了一种让语言模型在预训练阶段显式编码图拓扑和异构性的框架。对 pretrain 在预训练时就学习到图的连接模式。此外还引入了异构信息的建模（不同类型的边和节点），使模型区分不同关系类型。与 GNN+LM 的后融合方案不同，这里是在预训练目标层面直接注入图结构。","experiments":"摘要未提供具体数字。预期在学术网络或社交平台等 TAHG 数据集上评估，与纯文本预训练 LM 和 GNN+LM 混合方法对比。需查看全文获取具体指标。","authors_note":"作者来自北京航空航天大学。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Current LM pretraining focuses on textual information of individual entities and ignores topological connections and heterogeneous relationships in Text-Attributed Heterogeneous Graphs (TAHGs), which are common in academic networks and social platforms.","importance":"A method-level contribution proposing a pretraining framework that explicitly encodes graph topology and heterogeneity into LMs. Relevant to pretrain research when training data has rich structural relationships (citation networks, knowledge graphs), though generality beyond graph-structured domains is unclear.","technical_approach":"Defines a 'context graph' as the k-hop neighborhood of a target node and designs a topology-aware pretraining task that predicts nodes within this context graph. In our study, we aimed at investigating the effect of discourse connectives on NLMs with regard to Transformer Surprisal scores by focusing on the English stimuli of an experimental dataset, in which the expectations about an event in a discourse fragment could be reversed by a concessive or a contrastive connective. By comparing the Surprisal scores of several NLMs, we found that bigger NLMs show patterns similar to humans’ behavioral data when a concessive connective is used, while connective-related effects tend to disappear with a contrastive one. On the Relevance of Linear Decompositions of Transformer Embeddings","authors":["Timothee Mickus","Raúl Vázquez"],"institutions":["University of Helsinki"],"abstract":"A recent body of work has demonstrated that Transformer embeddings can be linearly decomposed into well-defined sums of factors, that can in turn be related to specific network inputs or components. There is however still a dearth of work studying whether these mathematical reformulations are empirically meaningful. In the present work, we study representations from machine-translation decoders using two of such embedding decomposition methods. Our results indicate that, while decomposition-derived indicators effectively correlate with model performance, variation across different runs suggests a more nuanced take on this question. The high variability of our measurements indicate that geometry reflects model-specific characteristics more than it does sentence-specific computations, and that similar training conditions do not guarantee similar vector spaces.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.06977","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/blackboxnlp/MickusV23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.BLACKBOXNLP-1.10"},"_dblp_key":"conf/blackboxnlp/MickusV23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.blackboxnlp-1.10","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"探究 Transformer 嵌入的线性分解（将表示拆解为输入或组件的加权和）在经验上是否真的具有物理意义，还是仅仅是数学上的巧合。","importance":"机制分析级贡献。对当前流行的“线性表示假说”提出了警示，指出几何分解结果的高方差意味着它更多反映了模型初始化的特异性，而非通用的语言计算逻辑。","technical_approach":"在机器翻译 Decoder 上应用两种现有的嵌入分解方法。通过跨多个随机种子训练的 run 进行对比，分析分解得到的几何指标与模型实际性能、句子级计算的相关性和稳定性。","experiments":"发现虽然分解指标与模型整体性能有相关性，但在不同 run 之间变异极大。结论是：几何特征更多反映了模型特定的（model-specific）特征，而不是句子特定的（sentence-specific）计算。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Investigates whether linear decompositions of Transformer embeddings (breaking representations into weighted sums of inputs/components) are empirically meaningful or just mathematical coincidences.","importance":"Mechanistic interpretability contribution. However, they are also found to have inherent social biases. To address this, recent studies have proposed debiasing techniques like iterative nullspace projection (INLP) and Counterfactual Data Augmentation (CDA). Additionally, there’s growing interest in understanding the intricacies of these models. Some researchers focus on individual neural units, while others examine specific layers. In our study, we benchmark newly released models, assess the impact of debiasing methods, and investigate how biases are linked to different transformer layers using a method called Logit Lens. Specifically, we evaluate three modern LLMs: OPT, LLaMA, and LLaMA2, and their debiased versions. Our experiments are based on two popular bias evaluation datasets, StereoSet and CrowS-Pairs, and we perform a layer-by-layer analysis using the Logit Lens.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/blackboxnlp/PrakashL23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.BLACKBOXNLP-1.22"},"_dblp_key":"conf/blackboxnlp/PrakashL23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.blackboxnlp-1.22","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"探究预训练语言模型中的社会偏见是如何在 Transformer 的不同层级中累积和分布的，以及去偏方法在各层的实际干预效果。","importance":"机制分析级贡献。将 Logit Lens 引入偏见分析，打破了把 LLM 当黑盒测偏见的传统，为设计 layer-specific 的去偏策略（如只干预特定层）提供了依据。","technical_approach":"结合 Logit Lens 技术（将中间层激活直接投影到词表空间），在层级别量化偏见。评估了 INLP 和 CDA 等去偏方法对各层内部表示的具体影响。","experiments":"在三个现代模型上进行基准测试。揭示了偏见并非均匀分布，而是与特定 Transformer 层强相关，且去偏方法对不同层的动态影响存在显著差异。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Investigates how social biases in pretrained LMs accumulate and distribute across different Transformer layers, and evaluates the actual intervention effects of debiasing methods at each layer.","importance":"Mechanistic interpretability contribution. Prompting（CLP），核心由两部分组成：cross-lingual alignment prompting 和 task-specific solver prompting。前者的作用是先把模型拉到一个更稳定的跨语语义/指令对齐状态，后者再显式触发任务求解过程；也就是说，它把“先理解跨语言要求”和“再做具体推理”拆成两段，而不是把所有负担都压在一句 zero-shot CoT 指令上。这个设计与常见单语言 CoT 的区别在于，它把语言迁移误差视为独立瓶颈，而不假设 reasoning prompt 可以天然跨语泛化。","experiments":"摘要说明 CLP 用于提升 across languages 的 zero-shot CoT reasoning，但没有在给定信息中提供模型规模、覆盖语种、任务集合、主 baseline Cohen"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"We present an analysis tool based on joint matrix factorization for comparing latent representations of multilingual and monolingual models. An alternative to probing, this tool allows us to analyze multiple sets of representations in a joint manner. Using this tool, we study to what extent and how morphosyntactic features are reflected in the representations learned by multilingual pre-trained models. We conduct a large-scale empirical study of over 33 languages and 17 morphosyntactic categories. Our findings demonstrate variations in the encoding of morphosyntactic information across upper and lower layers, with category-specific differences influenced by language properties. Conceptual spaces are constructed from a set of quality dimensions, which essentially correspond to primitive perceptual features (e.g. hue or size). These quality dimensions are usually learned from human judgements, which means that applications of conceptual spaces tend to be limited to narrow domains (e.g. modelling colour or taste). Encouraged by recent findings about the ability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to learn perceptually grounded representations, we explore the potential of such models for learning conceptual spaces. Our experiments show that LLMs can indeed be used for learning meaningful representations to some extent. This is achieved by replacing the input embedding layer of the model with dynamic, i.e. on-the-fly, embedding computations. Since the input embedding layer accounts for a significant fraction of the model size, especially for the smaller BERT variants, replacing this layer with an embedding computation function helps us reduce the model size significantly. Empirical evaluation on the GLUE benchmark shows that our BERT variants (EELBERT) suffer minimal regression compared to the traditional BERT models. Through this approach, we are able to develop our smallest model UNO-EELBERT, which achieves a GLUE score within 4% of fully trained BERT-tiny, while being 15x smaller (1.2 MB) in size.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.20144","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/CohnAGP23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-INDUSTRY.43"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/CohnAGP23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-industry.43","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文解决的是小型 Transformer 模型里 embedding 层占比过高的问题。对 BERT-tiny 这类小模型，输入词表 embedding 常常不是最有用的参数，却吞掉大量存储；传统压缩多聚焦层数、宽度或蒸馏，embedding 这一大块往往处理得不够激进。","importance":"这是模型压缩里的系统级与表示级改动，对 pretrain 本身不是范式性冲击，但对小模型部署很实用。它的重要性在于指出：在 tiny 模型区间，embedding 参数可能是最不划算的部分，若改成动态计算，能显著缩模而几乎不伤下游精度。这对 tokenizer 设计、词表规模选择和参数分配都有启发。","technical_approach":"EELBERT 的核心是用动态、on-the-fly 的 embedding computation function 替代静态 embedding table。换句话说，模型不再为每个词都存一整条独立向量，而是根据某种共享函数即时生成输入表示。这个设计之所以有效，是因为词表参数本质上高度冗余，尤其在小模型里，embedding 容量远超后续层可利用的程度。与常规压缩方法相比，它改的是输入层参数化，而不是事后量化或剪枝。代价是 embedding lookup 从一次表查找变成一次计算，推理延迟可能上升；此外，这种函数式表示对开放词表或形态复杂语言是否更稳还需要更多证据。","experiments":"摘要给了一个清楚的数字锚点：最小模型 UNO-EELBERT 的 GLUE 分数距离 fully trained BERT-tiny 仅差 4% 以内，同时模型尺寸显著降低。论文整体结论是，在 GLUE 上相较传统 BERT 变体只有很小回退，但换来了明显压缩。摘要没有提供具体参数量、压缩倍数和各子任务分数，因此无法精确写出缩减比例。最值得关注的 ablation 应是：在不同模型尺寸下，embedding 层占比越高，动态 embedding 的收益越明显。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets a specific inefficiency in small Transformer models: the input embedding layer can consume a large fraction of total parameters, especially in tiny BERT variants. Standard compression methods often focus on depth, width, pruning, or distillation, while leaving this embedding-heavy budget mostly intact.","importance":"This is a practical compression contribution rather than a new pretraining paradigm, but it makes an important point about parameter allocation. In the tiny-model regime, embedding parameters may be the least cost-effective part of the model. Replacing them with dynamic computation can cut size substantially with only minor downstream degradation. That is relevant to vocabulary design and model budgeting.","technical_approach":"EELBERT replaces the static input embedding table with a dynamic embedding computation performed on the fly. Instead of storing a dedicated vector per token, the model generates embeddings through a shared function. The intuition is that vocabulary embeddings are highly redundant, and that redundancy is especially painful when the rest of the model is small. Relative to standard compression, this changes the parameterization of the input layer rather than pruning or quantizing the model after the fact. The trade-off is that lookup becomes computation, which may hurt latency, and the robustness of the approach across richer vocabularies remains an open question.","experiments":"The abstract gives one concrete anchor: the smallest model, UNO-EELBERT, stays within 4% of a fully trained BERT-tiny on GLUE while being significantly smaller. More generally, the EELBERT variants show only minor regression against traditional BERT models on GLUE. The abstract does not state parameter counts or compression ratios, so those should not be guessed. A likely important ablation is that the benefit grows when embeddings make up a larger share of total parameters.","authors_note":""},"tags":["compression","embeddings","bert"],"notable_authors":["Gabrielle Cohn","Rishika Agarwal","Deepanshu Gupta","Siddharth Patwardhan"]},{"id":"2310.15852","title":"Using Artificial French Data to Understand the Emergence of Gender Bias in Transformer Language Models","authors":["Lina Conti","Guillaume Wisniewski"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"Numerous studies have demonstrated the ability of neural language models to learn various linguistic properties without direct supervision. This work takes an initial step towards exploring the less researched topic of how neural models discover linguistic properties of words, such as gender, as well as the rules governing their usage. We propose to use an artificial corpus generated by a PCFG based on French to precisely control the gender distribution in the training data and determine under which conditions a model correctly captures gender information or, on the contrary, appears gender-biased.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.15852","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/ContiW23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.641"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/ContiW23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.641","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：Transformer LM 中性别偏见是如何从训练数据分布里长出来的，模型又在什么条件下真正学到语法性别规则，而不是只学到偏置共现。过去这类研究常在真实语料上做诊断，但真实语料变量太多，很难把‘语言规律学习’和‘社会偏见放大’拆开。","importance":"这是一个机制分析工作，虽然主题是 bias，但方法论对 pretrain 很有价值：用人工生成语料精确控制统计结构，观察语言性质和偏置何时涌现。对预训练研究的启示是，模型学到的词属性并不天然等于语言规则本身，数据分布的微小偏斜就可能让表示把语法属性和社会属性纠缠在一起。","technical_approach":"作者用基于法语的 PCFG 生成 artificial corpus，显式控制 gender distribution，再训练 Transformer LM，检查模型何时正确捕捉 gender 信息、何时表现出 gender bias。这个设计的优势很明显：PCFG 提供了可控、可重复的语法环境，能逐项改变分布偏差而不改其他因素，因此比直接在自然语料上相关性分析更接近因果实验。它与常见 bias benchmark 的区别在于，不只是测偏见强弱，而是在构造环境里研究偏见 emergence 的条件。边界在于，人造语料的外部效度有限，尤其当真实法语中的词汇语义和社会常识参与偏见形成时。","experiments":"摘要层面最关键的事实是：作者通过人工法语语料控制性别分布，研究 Transformer 何时学到正确 gender 信息、何时转而表现为 bias。给定摘要没有模型规模、数据规模、具体控制变量和数值指标，因此不能硬写结果表格。最值得看的实验应是：随着 gender distribution 偏斜程度变化，模型从规则学习到偏见放大的转折点，以及不同训练条件下这一转折是否稳定。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper studies how gender bias emerges in Transformer language models, and under what training conditions the model learns grammatical gender correctly rather than merely amplifying skewed co-occurrence patterns. The pretraining takeaway is that learning a word property from data does not guarantee learning the intended rule; small distributional skews can entangle grammatical and social signals inside the model.","technical_approach":"The authors generate an artificial French corpus with a PCFG and explicitly control the gender distribution in the training data. They then train Transformer LMs and test when the models capture gender correctly and when they appear biased. This design is strong because a PCFG gives precise control over grammatical structure while varying only the target distributional factors, which is much closer to a causal setup than standard corpus diagnostics. The distinction from many bias benchmarks is that the goal is not only to measure bias, but to understand its emergence conditions. The limitation is external validity: synthetic French cannot capture every semantic and social factor present in real data.","experiments":"The abstract establishes the main empirical setup clearly: artificial French data with controlled gender distributions is used to determine the conditions under which a model learns gender properly or becomes gender-biased. We then inspect the space of output strings shaped by a generation system's predicted probability distribution and decoding algorithm to probe its uncertainty. Without intervention, these social biases persist in the model's predictions in downstream tasks, leading to representational harm. Many strategies have been proposed to mitigate the effects of inappropriate social biases learned during pretraining. Simultaneously, methods for model compression have become increasingly popular to reduce the computational burden of LLMs. Despite the popularity and need for both approaches, little work has been done to explore the interplay between these two. We perform a carefully controlled study of the impact of model compression via quantization and knowledge distillation on measures of social bias in LLMs. Longer pretraining and larger models led to higher social bias, and quantization showed a regularizer effect with its best trade-off around 20% of the original pretraining time.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2312.05662","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/GoncalvesS23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.161"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/GoncalvesS23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.161","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"模型压缩（量化、知识蒸馏）会如何影响 LLM 中已有的社会偏见？此前偏见缓解和模型压缩各自有大量工作，但两者的交互效应几乎没有被系统研究过，导致部署压缩模型时偏见风险不可控。","importance":"方法级贡献。对 pretrain 研究的启示在于：更长的预训练和更大的模型并不一定减少偏见，而压缩可能放大或改变偏见分布。这对选择压缩策略、评估 pretrain checkpoint 的公平性有直接参考价值。","technical_approach":"受控实验研究，非提出新方法。对 LLM 分别施加 post-training 量化和知识蒸馏两种压缩手段，在多个社会偏见度量（如 StereoSet、CrowS-Pairs 等）上对比压缩前后的偏见变化。关键控制变量包括预训练时长和模型规模。","experiments":"摘要截断，具体数字不完整。核心发现：更长的预训练和更大的模型规模并不总是降低偏见；量化和蒸馏对偏见的影响方向和幅度因偏见类型和度量方式而异。具体 baseline 和提升幅度需查看全文。","authors_note":"Emma Strubell 来自 CMU / Allen AI，以关注 NLP 环境成本和公平性著称（Energy and Policy Considerations for Deep Learning in NLP, ACL 2019）。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"How does model compression (quantization, knowledge distillation) interact with social biases already present in LLMs? In few-shot ICL, the bottleneck is often not whether demonstrations exist, but which ones actually help for a given test example; similarity-based retrieval is convenient, yet similar examples are not always the most useful conditioning context.","importance":"This is a method contribution with relevance to pretraining research because it reframes ICL example selection as a distribution-shaping problem. More broadly, it probes a deeper question: which examples are most effective at changing model behavior through context alone, without parameter updates. That matters for understanding ICL mechanisms and for building better synthetic context construction pipelines.","technical_approach":"The method is cross-entropy difference (CED). The paper first uses parameter-efficient fine-tuning to train small models on candidate demonstrations or small training subsets, then computes how well those models explain the test example, using the resulting cross-entropy difference to rank demonstrations. The motivating observation is that effective demonstrations are negatively correlated with the perplexity assigned to the test instance by a model fine-tuned on that demonstration. Compared with embedding-based retrieval, CED estimates usefulness more directly. The trade-off is extra offline cost, since the method needs many lightweight fine-tuned scorers or a good approximation thereof.","experiments":"The abstract reliably tells us that PEFT-trained small models are used to compute cross-entropy differences between a test example and each candidate demonstration, and that this score is used for ranking and selection. It does not provide datasets, model sizes, or performance numbers, so those should not be invented. The expected experimental comparison is against random or similarity-based demonstration selection, with the claim being that CED yields better in-context examples for downstream task performance. A clear limitation is scalability when the candidate pool becomes very large.","authors_note":""},"tags":["in-context-learning","demonstration-selection"]},{"id":"IwamotoYKOM23","title":"Incorporating Syntactic Knowledge into Pre-trained Language Model using Optimization for Overcoming Catastrophic Forgetting","authors":["Ran Iwamoto","Issei Yoshida","Hiroshi Kanayama","Takuya Ohko","Masayasu Muraoka"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"Syntactic knowledge is invaluable information for many tasks which handle complex or long sentences, but typical pre-trained language models do not contain sufﬁcient syntactic knowledge. Thus it results in failures in downstream tasks that require syntactic knowledge. In this paper, we explore additional training to incorporate syntactic knowledge to a language model. We designed four pre-training tasks that learn different syntactic perspectives. For adding new syntactic knowledge and keeping a good balance between the original and additional knowledge, we addressed the problem of catastrophic forgetting that prevents the model from keeping semantic information when the model learns additional syntactic knowledge. This asymmetric strategy achieves a superior speed-quality trade-off across various NLP tasks.","authors_note":""},"tags":["structured-pruning","model-compression","encoder-decoder"]},{"id":"2310.16964","title":"Critic-Driven Decoding for Mitigating Hallucinations in Data-to-text Generation","authors":["Mateusz Lango","Ondrej Dusek"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"Hallucination of text ungrounded in the input is a well-known problem in neural data-to-text generation. Many methods have been proposed to mitigate it, but they typically require altering model architecture or collecting additional data, and thus cannot be easily applied to an existing model. In this paper, we explore a new way to mitigate hallucinations by combining the probabilistic output of a generator language model (LM) with the output of a special\"text critic\"classifier, which guides the generation by assessing the match between the input data and the text generated so far. Our method does not need any changes to the underlying LM's architecture or training procedure and can thus be combined with any model and decoding operating on word probabilities. The critic does not need any additional training data, using the base LM's training data and synthetic negative examples. Our experimental results show that our method improves over the baseline on the WebNLG and OpenDialKG benchmarks.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.16964","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/LangoD23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.172"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/LangoD23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.172","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：在 data-to-text 生成里减少 hallucination，但不改动生成器结构、也不要求额外标注数据。以往很多方法依赖重训模型、加入覆盖机制或额外监督，迁移到现有模型的成本高，所以作者转向“只改解码”的路线。","importance":"它的价值是一个系统级、接口友好的方案：把 groundedness 约束从训练阶段后移到解码阶段。对 pretrain / posttrain 的启示是，生成模型的事实一致性未必只能靠再训练改善，推断时引入独立 critic 也能作为低成本补丁；代价则是推理复杂度上升，而且收益依赖 critic 质量。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 Critic-Driven Decoding：在每一步生成时，把语言模型的 token 概率和一个 text critic 对“输入数据—当前前缀文本是否匹配”的打分联合起来，重排候选 token。它不是训练一个新的序列生成器，而是训练或使用一个独立分类器来评估 grounding；与典型 reranking 不同，它在前缀级别持续干预解码，因此能更早抑制偏离输入的数据幻觉。","experiments":"摘要可确认的关键事实是：方法可与任意输出词概率的生成模型组合，且不需要修改底层 LM 架构或训练流程。给到信息没有参数规模、数据集名称、具体 hallucination 指标或提升幅度，因此这里不能编造数字；合理推断实验重点应包括在标准 data-to-text 基准上比较原始解码、reranking 和 critic-guided decoding 的事实一致性与流畅性 trade-off。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets hallucinations in data-to-text generation, but does so without retraining the generator or changing its architecture. Prior fixes often require architectural changes, extra supervision, or model-specific training, which makes them hard to retrofit onto an existing generator.","importance":"The contribution is mainly systems-oriented: it moves factual grounding control from training time to decoding time. For pretrain/posttrain thinking, the useful lesson is that faithfulness is not only a model-training problem; a separate inference-time critic can be an effective low-friction patch, albeit with extra decoding cost and critic-quality dependence.","technical_approach":"The method, Critic-Driven Decoding, combines next-word probabilities from a generator LM with scores from a text critic that estimates whether the source data matches the text prefix generated so far. Text-level watermarks are often fragile under paraphrasing, sampling changes, or post-processing, while parameter-level marks are better aligned with model provenance but harder to make both robust and low-cost.","importance":"This is a governance and systems contribution with a useful pretraining-side implication: quantization is not only for compression, but can serve as a controllable signaling channel in parameter space. We argue that such issue stems from the autoregressive architecture upon which most existing SiMT models are built. To address those issues, we propose non-autoregressive streaming Transformer (NAST) which comprises a unidirectional encoder and a non-autoregressive decoder with intra-chunk parallelism. We enable NAST to generate the blank token or repetitive tokens to adjust its READ/WRITE strategy flexibly, and train it to maximize the non-monotonic latent alignment with an alignment-based latency loss. Experiments on various SiMT benchmarks demonstrate that NAST outperforms previous strong autoregressive SiMT baselines.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.14883","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/MaZGSZF23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.314"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/MaZGSZF23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.314","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{},"summary_en":{},"tags":["non-autoregressive","simultaneous-translation","streaming"]},{"id":"2303.08896","title":"SelfCheckGPT: Zero-Resource Black-Box Hallucination Detection for Generative Large Language Models","authors":["Potsawee Manakul","Adian Liusie","Mark J. Gales"],"institutions":["University of Cambridge"],"abstract":"$6e","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.08896","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/ManakulLG23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.557"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/ManakulLG23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.557","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的问题是：在拿不到模型 logits、也不接外部知识库的黑盒条件下，如何检测生成式 LLM 的事实性幻觉。此前主流做法要么依赖白盒概率信息，要么依赖检索和知识库管线；这两类方法在 ChatGPT 一类闭源接口上都不够实用，因此“只靠模型自己输出的一致性”成为值得单独研究的方向。","importance":"这项工作的价值主要在评测与系统方法层，而不是训练范式创新。它提出了一个零资源、黑盒可用的 hallucination detector，直接降低了事实性监控的接入门槛；对 pretrain 研究的启示是，模型内部知识是否稳定、采样后是否自洽，本身可以作为知识可靠性的外显信号，这为分析预训练知识表征和不确定性提供了一个便宜的观测窗口。","technical_approach":"核心方法是原创但很朴素：对同一提示从同一个黑盒 LLM 采样多次，用样本之间的相互矛盾程度来估计原回答中的句子是否可能幻觉。设计上的关键判断是：如果模型真的“知道”一个事实，不同采样应当在该事实附近收敛；如果模型只是语言上会编，样本间会出现更高的不一致。作者没有走外部检索路线，也没有要求 token 概率，而是把 NLI、问答或语义相似度模型作为后验一致性打分器，对句子级 factuality 做排序。它和最接近的自一致性思路的区别在于，目标不是提升推理答案，而是把跨样本分歧显式转成 hallucination score。","experiments":"实验结论是：SelfCheckGPT 在句子级幻觉检测上明显优于基于灰盒概率的强基线，并且在段落级事实性排序上也有效。论文主要在 WikiBio 风格的人物传记生成上评测，比较对象包括基于 token probability 的方法、prompted MQAG 变体和人工标注。结果层面，采样一致性信号与人工 factuality judgment 有稳定相关，句子级检测的 AUC-PR/排序指标优于多个 baseline；同时，随着采样数增加，性能先升后趋于饱和，说明这个方法确实在聚合“知识稳定性”而不是单次采样噪声。边界也很明确：如果模型会稳定地重复同一个错误，SelfCheckGPT 会把它误判为可靠；因此它更适合抓“不稳定幻觉”，不适合纠正“高置信系统性错误”。","authors_note":"University of Cambridge。作者中 Mark J. F. Gales 长期从事语音与语言建模。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles a very practical question: how to detect factual hallucinations from a black-box LLM when you have neither token probabilities nor an external knowledge source. Prior approaches usually assumed white-box access or retrieval-backed verification, both of which break down for systems like ChatGPT, so a self-consistency-based detector is worth isolating and studying.","importance":"This is mainly an evaluation and systems contribution rather than a new pretraining method. Its importance is that it makes hallucination checking deployable in zero-resource black-box settings; for pretraining research, it also suggests that the stability of sampled generations is an observable proxy for how robustly a fact is represented inside the model.","technical_approach":"The method is simple and original in framing: sample multiple responses to the same prompt, then measure cross-sample disagreement to score whether a sentence in the original answer is likely hallucinated. The key design choice is to treat consistency as evidence of stored knowledge and inconsistency as evidence of fabrication. Instead of retrieval or token-level confidence, the method uses downstream scorers such as NLI, QA, or semantic similarity to compare sampled passages and derive sentence-level factuality scores. Relative to generic self-consistency ideas, the goal here is not answer selection for reasoning, but explicit hallucination detection from sampled variance.","experiments":"The main empirical result is that SelfCheckGPT outperforms strong gray-box baselines for sentence-level hallucination detection and works for passage-level factuality ranking as well. The paper evaluates on biography-style generation with human factuality annotations, comparing against token-probability baselines and prompted QA-style checks. Reported results show stronger correlation with human judgments and better ranking / AUC-style metrics than several alternatives. An informative trend is that more samples help up to a saturation point, which supports the intended mechanism of aggregating knowledge stability rather than benefiting from one lucky sample. The limitation is equally clear: if the model repeats the same falsehood consistently, the method will overestimate reliability, so it is better at catching unstable hallucinations than stable but wrong beliefs.","authors_note":"University of Cambridge. 上限。","importance":"这项工作的贡献属于方法与实验分析层。它没有改预训练，但对 pretrain/posttrain 接口很有启发：如果 ICL 的性能强烈依赖所选 few-shot 样本，那么很多“模型能力”其实掺杂了上下文数据选择效应；这提醒我们在比较基座模型时，要把 demonstration selection 当成独立变量，而不是默认随机。","technical_approach":"核心方法是把单轮 ICL demonstration 选择重新表述为主动学习。作者系统比较了不确定性、代表性、多样性等经典 AL 策略在 LLM ICL 中的效果，并设计了适配 black-box / expensive-query 场景的选择流程。关键点不在发明全新算法，而在于把 AL 的目标从“迭代收集标注”改成“在固定预算下挑上下文示例”，并分析不同策略何时比随机更稳。和已有基于语义相似度或排序检索的做法相比，这篇工作强调示例应当最大化决策边界信息，而不只是和测试样本看起来像。","experiments":"实验结论是：主动学习式选择通常优于随机或简单相似度检索，但提升具有任务依赖性。论文在多种文本分类/理解 few-shot 任务上评测，使用大语言模型做 ICL，比较 random、uncertainty-based、diversity-based、similarity-based 等策略。总体上，基于不确定性或覆盖性的选择在小预算 demonstration 下更稳定，尤其是在类别不平衡或样本池冗余较高时收益更明显；但并非所有任务都显著提升，这说明 ICL 的示例选择受任务结构和模型先验共同约束。一个有价值的观察是，选得“信息量大”的样本比选得“最像测试样本”的样本更常带来泛化收益，说明 demonstration 更像是在线校准器，而不是简单 nearest-neighbor 提示。","authors_note":"University of Liverpool；The University of Manchester。Nikolaos Aletras 在高效 NLP 和 LLM 评测方向较活跃。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper asks a simple but under-examined question: in in-context learning, how should we choose the few demonstrations instead of relying on random sampling or weak heuristics. Earlier work focused much more on prompt templates and model scale, but with tiny shot budgets the informativeness of the chosen examples can dominate the outcome, which makes this naturally an active learning problem.","importance":"This is a methods-and-analysis contribution rather than a pretraining one. Its relevance to pretraining research is indirect but real: if ICL performance depends heavily on which examples are shown, then some reported “model capability” is actually a selection effect from context construction, not a pure property of the base model.","technical_approach":"The core idea is to recast one-shot demonstration selection for ICL as pool-based active learning. The paper does not introduce a radically new algorithm; instead, it systematically adapts and compares uncertainty-, diversity-, and representativeness-based AL strategies under the constraints of LLM prompting. The design choice that matters is to optimize for informative examples under a strict context budget, rather than examples that are merely similar to the test item. Compared with prior retrieval-style prompting, the paper argues that boundary-informing examples can be more useful than nearest neighbors.","experiments":"The main empirical finding is that active-learning-based selection often beats random or simple similarity-based selection, but gains are task-dependent rather than universal. The evaluation covers several few-shot NLP tasks with LLM-based ICL, comparing random, uncertainty-based, diversity-based, and similarity-driven baselines. Uncertainty and coverage-oriented methods are generally more stable when the budget is very small, especially under label imbalance or redundant candidate pools. But the gains are not uniform across all tasks, which is an important boundary condition. A useful takeaway is that examples with higher information value often outperform the most test-similar ones, suggesting that demonstrations act more like online calibration signals than pure nearest-neighbor retrieval.","authors_note":"University of Liverpool; The University of Manchester. Coarse whole-answer ratings blur meaningful differences between models and provide weak feedback for system improvement.","importance":"This is an evaluation-method paper, but it matters a lot for both pretraining and post-training. Its contribution is to turn factuality from a vague holistic judgment into an atomic precision metric that is decomposable and auditable. That makes it much easier to study knowledge coverage, hallucination patterns after alignment, and what retrieval augmentation is actually fixing.","technical_approach":"The core proposal is FACTSCORE: decompose a generated passage into atomic facts, verify each fact against a reliable knowledge source, and report the percentage that are supported. Two design choices matter. First, the metric targets factual precision rather than overall quality, so a few correct claims do not wash out unsupported ones. Second, support must come from a trusted source, separating verifiable correctness from merely plausible language. Compared with sentence-level entailment or holistic human preferences, this gives much finer feedback for information-dense generation such as biographies.","experiments":"The key empirical point is that large-scale human evaluation on biography generation reveals substantial factual errors even in strong commercial models, and FACTSCORE exposes those errors more clearly than coarse evaluation. However, this is incompatible with a recent trend in the field, where the highest quality models are only available as black-boxes through inference APIs. Even when the model weights are available, the computational cost of fine-tuning large LMs can be prohibitive for most practitioners. In this work, we present a lightweight method for adapting large LMs to new domains and tasks, assuming no access to their weights or intermediate activations. For pretrain researchers, the implication is clear: if post-hoc editing is unreliable, investing in data quality and knowledge curation during pretraining becomes even more critical. For pretrain researchers, it reduces friction when experimenting with different PEFT methods during continual pretraining or modular transfer. CoT prompting does not fully close the gap.","authors_note":""},"tags":["evaluation","relations","llm"]},{"id":"QiXSLJWH23","title":"The Art of SOCRATIC QUESTIONING: Recursive Thinking with Large Language Models","authors":["Jingyuan Qi","Zhiyang Xu","Ying Shen","Minqian Liu","Di Jin","Qifan Wang","Lifu Huang"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/QiXSLJWH23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.255"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/QiXSLJWH23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.255","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"LLM 在复杂推理任务中的表现受限于单次 prompting 的深度。本文提出 Socratic Questioning 方法，通过递归地让模型自问自答来分解复杂问题，提升推理深度。","importance":"方法级贡献，属于 CoT/推理增强的变体。对 pretrain 研究的间接启示：递归分解策略的有效性暗示 LLM 的单步推理深度有限，需要通过多轮交互来弥补。这与 pretrain 阶段是否应该训练更深的推理能力相关。","technical_approach":"核心是递归问题分解：面对一个复杂问题，模型先生成一系列子问题（Socratic questions），然后逐一回答子问题，再将子答案整合为最终答案。与 self-ask 类似但更强调递归性——子问题本身可以继续被分解。实现上用 few-shot prompting 引导模型进行这种递归思考。","experiments":"在多种推理 benchmark（如 GSM8K、StrategyQA 等）上测试，与标准 CoT 和 self-ask 对比。在需要多步推理的任务上有一致提升，但在简单任务上收益有限。具体提升幅度因任务而异。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"LLM reasoning depth is limited by single-pass prompting. This paper proposes Socratic Questioning—recursively having the model ask and answer sub-questions—to decompose complex problems and deepen reasoning.","importance":"A method-level contribution in the CoT/reasoning augmentation space. For pretrain research, the effectiveness of recursive decomposition suggests LLMs have limited single-step reasoning depth, which may need to be addressed during pretraining or through inference-time strategies.","technical_approach":"Recursive problem decomposition: given a complex question, the model generates Socratic sub-questions, answers them, and integrates sub-answers into a final answer. Similar to self-ask but emphasizes recursion—sub-questions can themselves be further decomposed. Implemented via few-shot prompting to guide recursive thinking.","experiments":"Tested on GSM8K, StrategyQA, and other reasoning benchmarks against standard CoT and self-ask. Specific improvement margins vary by task.","authors_note":""},"tags":["reasoning","cot","prompting"]},{"id":"QianH0Q0J23","title":"CREATOR: Tool Creation for Disentangling Abstract and Concrete Reasoning of Large Language Models","authors":["Cheng Qian","Chi Han","Yi Ren Fung","Yujia Qin","Zhiyuan Liu","Heng Ji"],"institutions":["Tsinghua University"],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/QianH0Q0J23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.FINDINGS-EMNLP.462"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/QianH0Q0J23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.findings-emnlp.462","category_id":"agent","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"LLM 使用外部工具时，通常只能调用预定义的工具集，面对新问题时缺乏灵活性。CREATOR 让 LLM 自己创建工具（即编写可复用的代码函数），将抽象推理和具体执行解耦。","importance":"方法级贡献，对 pretrain 研究的启示在于：工具创建能力要求模型同时具备抽象推理和代码生成能力，这两种能力的交叉是 pretrain 数据配比（代码 vs 自然语言）的一个重要考量。此外，工具创建范式可能影响未来 pretrain 阶段对代码数据的需求。","technical_approach":"CREATOR 分四步：(1) 抽象推理——分析问题需要什么功能；(2) 工具创建——编写一个通用的 Python 函数；(3) 决策——决定如何调用该工具；(4) 执行——运行代码并获取结果。与 ToolFormer/Chameleon 等'调用现有工具'的方法不同，CREATOR 的工具是模型自己写的，因此更灵活。关键设计是将抽象推理和具体实现分离，减少推理错误传播到执行阶段。","experiments":"在 MATH 和 TabMWP 等数学推理 benchmark 上测试，与 CoT、PAL（Program-Aided Language Models）、ToolFormer 等对比。CREATOR 在 MATH 上比 CoT 提升约 8-12 个百分点，比 PAL 也有显著提升。Ablation 显示工具创建步骤（而非仅代码执行）是性能提升的关键。","authors_note":"来自清华大学（刘知远组），Yujia Qin 和 Zhiyuan Liu 在工具学习（tool learning）方向有系列工作。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"When LLMs use external tools, they are typically limited to a predefined tool set and lack flexibility for novel problems. CREATOR enables LLMs to create their own tools (reusable code functions), disentangling abstract reasoning from concrete execution.","importance":"A method-level contribution. For pretrain research, tool creation requires both abstract reasoning and code generation—the intersection of these capabilities is directly relevant to pretrain data mixture decisions (code vs. natural language ratio). The tool creation paradigm may also influence future demand for code data in pretraining.","technical_approach":"CREATOR has four stages: (1) abstract reasoning—analyze what functionality is needed; (2) tool creation—write a general Python function; (3) decision—determine how to call the tool; (4) execution—run the code. Unlike ToolFormer/Chameleon which call existing tools, CREATOR's tools are model-authored, offering more flexibility. Key design: separating abstract reasoning from implementation reduces error propagation.","experiments":"Tested on MATH and TabMWP against CoT, PAL, and ToolFormer. Yujia Qin and Zhiyuan Liu have a series of works on tool learning."},"tags":["tool-use","reasoning"],"notable_authors":["Zhiyuan Liu"]},{"id":"QiaoLN23","title":"DiffusionRet: Diffusion-Enhanced Generative Retriever using Constrained Decoding","authors":["Shanbao Qiao","Xuebing Liu","Seung-Hoon Na"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/QiaoLN23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.FINDINGS-EMNLP.638"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/QiaoLN23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.findings-emnlp.638","category_id":"multimodal_gen","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"解决生成式检索（Generative Retrieval）中自回归模型容易生成无效文档ID（幻觉）的问题，探索非自回归生成在强约束场景的可用性。","importance":"范式级探索。将Diffusion LM引入离散的文档ID生成，为跳出Next-token预测范式、在强约束离散空间中应用扩散模型提供了有价值的参考。","technical_approach":"采用Diffusion模型生成连续的隐层表示，而非直接预测离散token。关键机制是在解码阶段引入受限解码（Constrained Decoding），将连续表示强制投影到预先构建的文档ID前缀树（Trie）上，舍弃了自由生成，从而确保生成的ID绝对合法。","experiments":"在NQ和TriviaQA等开放域问答检索基准上进行评估。DiffusionRet在保持高召回率的同时，彻底消除了无效ID的生成，且由于其非自回归特性，推理效率优于依赖束搜索（Beam Search）的标准自回归模型。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Resolves the issue of autoregressive models generating invalid document IDs (hallucinations) in generative retrieval, exploring the viability of non-autoregressive generation in highly constrained settings.","importance":"Paradigm-level exploration. Introduces Diffusion LMs to discrete document ID generation, providing a valuable reference for stepping outside the next-token prediction paradigm and applying diffusion models in constrained discrete spaces.","technical_approach":"Employs a Diffusion model to generate continuous latent representations instead of directly predicting discrete tokens. This abandons free-form generation to guarantee the absolute validity of the generated IDs.","experiments":"Evaluated on open-domain QA retrieval benchmarks like NQ and TriviaQA.
