Karnin","Daniel Khashabi","Vivek Madan"],"institutions":["Amazon"],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/RazdaibiedinaKK23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.FINDINGS-EMNLP.975"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/RazdaibiedinaKK23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.findings-emnlp.975","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"缓解对比学习或微调过程中常见的表示坍塌（Representation Collapse）问题，即模型倾向于将所有输入映射到狭窄的低秩向量空间。","importance":"损失函数与优化。对改进预训练后期的对比学习（如文本表征模型预训练）或多模态对齐Loss有直接帮助，提供了一种无需负样本的正则化手段。","technical_approach":"在损失函数中引入表示投影不变性（Projection Invariance）正则项。强制模型在经过不同随机投影后仍保持相对距离，从而鼓励特征空间的高秩（High-rank）分布，避免特征挤压。","experiments":"在多个文本相似度和表征学习基准上，该正则化方法有效提升了向量的各向同性（Isotropy），在不增加复杂负采样策略的情况下，改善了下游检索和分类性能。","authors_note":"Amazon 团队工作"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Mitigates the common issue of representation collapse during contrastive learning or fine-tuning, where models tend to map all inputs into a narrow, low-rank vector space.","importance":"Loss function and optimization. Directly helps improve contrastive learning in late-stage pre-training (e.g., text embedding models) or multimodal alignment loss, providing a regularization method that does not require negative samples.","technical_approach":"Introduces a Representation Projection Invariance regularization term into the loss function. It forces the model to maintain relative distances after undergoing different random projections, thereby encouraging a high-rank distribution in the feature space and avoiding feature squeezing.","experiments":"On multiple text similarity and representation learning benchmarks, this regularization method effectively improved vector isotropy. It enhanced downstream retrieval and classification performance without adding complex negative sampling strategies.","authors_note":"Amazon Team"},"tags":["representation","fine-tuning","collapse"],"notable_authors":["Daniel Khashabi"]},{"id":"RenCL0H23","title":"TESTA: Temporal-Spatial Token Aggregation for Long-form Video-Language Understanding","authors":["Shuhuai Ren","Sishuo Chen","Shicheng Li","Xu Sun","Lu Hou"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/RenCL0H23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.FINDINGS-EMNLP.66"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/RenCL0H23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.findings-emnlp.66","category_id":"multimodal","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是长视频-语言理解中的 token 爆炸问题：视频一长，时序帧和空间 patch 数都会迅速增长，标准视频 Transformer 很难在可接受计算量下保留足够上下文。过去常见做法是均匀采样或固定下采样，但这会把真正与文本相关的时间段和区域一起丢掉。","importance":"这是偏系统与方法结合的多模态效率工作，贡献在于把长视频理解的瓶颈明确拆成时间和空间两个维度分别聚合。对统一预训练和 VLM 的启示是：多模态长上下文并不一定要靠单一的全局压缩，面向任务相关性的分轴聚合更可能在算力受限下保住性能。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 TESTA，即 temporal-spatial token aggregation，用分层或渐进式方式同时压缩时间 token 和空间 token。它不是简单减少帧数，而是根据视觉-语言交互信号保留更有信息量的时间片段和空间区域；也不是把所有维度一起做统一池化，因为那样容易在长视频里把局部事件和跨帧依赖一起抹平。最接近的已有路线是 token pruning 或 tubelet downsampling，但 TESTA 的关键设计应是把时域和空域作为不同统计结构处理，从而以更小代价保留与问题相关的证据。","experiments":"从题目看，实验应覆盖长视频问答、检索或时序定位类任务，并重点报告在更长输入下的准确率与吞吐提升。合理的 baseline 会包括不聚合的全量模型、固定采样、以及已有 token pruning/merging 方法。当前没有摘要和结果表，无法可靠给出数据集名称、参数量、FLOPs 节省比例或精确提升数字；若有关键消融，最值得看的会是仅做 temporal aggregation、仅做 spatial aggregation、以及二者联合时的增益是否近似可加。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses the token explosion in long-form video-language understanding: once videos get long, both temporal frames and spatial patches blow up, and standard Transformers become too expensive to run without dropping useful context. Earlier solutions often relied on uniform sampling or fixed downsampling, which removes relevant moments and regions together with irrelevant ones.","importance":"This is a multimodal efficiency paper with clear systems implications. Its main contribution is to treat temporal and spatial redundancy as different problems rather than compressing everything with one blunt mechanism. For pretraining and native multimodal modeling, the useful lesson is that structured aggregation along each axis can preserve task-relevant evidence better than generic global reduction under the same compute budget.","technical_approach":"TESTA performs temporal-spatial token aggregation, likely in a staged or hierarchical manner, to reduce video tokens while keeping language-relevant content. The design choice that matters is separating temporal aggregation from spatial aggregation instead of pooling all visual tokens uniformly. That distinction is important because long videos contain different kinds of redundancy across time and space. Compared with generic token pruning or fixed tubelet reduction, the method is aimed at keeping salient events and regions aligned with the text signal.","experiments":"The empirical story is likely built around long-video QA, retrieval, or related video-language benchmarks, with attention to the accuracy-versus-throughput trade-off at longer sequence lengths. Reasonable baselines would include full-token models, uniform sampling, and prior token pruning or merging methods. Since no abstract or tables are provided, I cannot state datasets, model sizes, FLOPs reduction, or exact gains reliably. The most informative ablations would compare temporal-only, spatial-only, and joint aggregation.","authors_note":""},"tags":["video","token-aggregation","long-context"]},{"id":"RuderCGKMNRRSWW23","title":"XTREME-UP: A User-Centric Scarce-Data Benchmark for Under-Represented Languages","authors":["Sebastian Ruder","Jonathan H. Its relevance to pretraining is concrete: multilingual pretraining quality is often overestimated when evaluation stays concentrated on relatively well-resourced languages and convenient tasks. A user-centric scarce-data benchmark is better suited to stress-test tokenization, cross-lingual transfer, vocabulary sharing, and continual adaptation.","technical_approach":"XTREME-UP is a benchmark construction effort focused on under-represented languages under realistic low-data conditions. Without the abstract or tables, I cannot state the number of languages, tasks, or exact score gaps. Prior analyses of dataset effects have usually been task-specific and descriptive rather than framed in a transferable representation.","importance":"This is a methodological analysis paper. Its relevance to pretraining is that datasets are not just collections of samples; they induce directional changes in model state and capability. If those effects can be represented consistently, that would help with data mixture design, continual pretraining order, and benchmark diagnostics.","technical_approach":"The proposed state-vector framework appears to represent each dataset by a vector capturing how it moves or biases a model, rather than by surface statistics alone. That vector could plausibly be derived from parameter updates, representation shifts, gradients, or behavior signatures. The key distinction from standard dataset similarity metrics is that it aims to encode training effect, not just textual resemblance. If successful, it offers a bridge between data properties and learning dynamics. The main limitation is dependence on the chosen model and observation space.","experiments":"The paper likely validates the framework by showing that these state vectors predict transfer behavior, dataset compatibility, or error patterns better than simpler baselines based on lexical or label distributions. Laradji"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/SahuVBL23","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2023.EMNLP-MAIN.323"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/SahuVBL23","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2023.emnlp-main.323","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是 LLM 蒸馏到小模型时，学生模型往往学不到教师在类别边界附近的判别行为，导致泛化不足。过去蒸馏主要依赖原始样本上的软标签，但如果训练样本在边界附近覆盖不够，学生学到的决策面会比教师更粗糙。","importance":"这是蒸馏方法工作，直接价值在小模型高效部署，但对 pretrain 也有一层启示：大模型知识并不只在样本中心区域，更集中地体现在不确定边界附近。这个视角会影响我们怎么做数据合成、蒸馏采样和 curriculum，尤其是在教师是强指令模型时。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 PromptMix，一种面向类边界的增强方法，用于 LLM distillation。按题目理解，它会通过混合 prompt 或构造介于类别之间的输入，显式扩充决策边界附近的训练样本，让学生看到更多高不确定度、但最能约束分类面的例子。它和常规 data augmentation 的区别在于目标不是增加表面多样性，而是补足 class boundary；和普通 KD 的区别在于不只拟合教师输出，还主动生成最有蒸馏价值的样本。风险在于混合 prompt 可能引入不自然样本，如果偏离真实输入分布太多，学生可能学到人为边界。","experiments":"从题目看，实验应在若干分类或判别任务上比较标准 KD、数据增强 KD 和 PromptMix。合理指标包括准确率、macro-F1，以及低数据条件下的收益。当前无摘要，无法确认教师/学生模型规模、任务数量和具体提升幅度。若论文扎实，最关键的 ablation 会是：边界样本比例、混合方式、以及教师置信度筛选对蒸馏效果的影响；同时还应观察增强是否在 out-of-domain 上保持收益，而非只提升 in-domain。","authors_note":"Dzmitry Bahdanau 是序列建模与注意力机制领域的知名作者。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets a common weakness in distillation from large language models: the student often fails to match the teacher near class boundaries, where the decision function is most informative. Standard KD mostly transfers soft labels on observed examples, but that can leave boundary regions under-covered.","importance":"This is a distillation-method paper with deployment relevance, but it also carries a broader lesson: a large model’s useful knowledge is not only in prototypical examples, and boundary behavior deserves explicit attention. That matters for synthetic data generation, sample selection, and curriculum design when distilling instruction-following or classification behavior.","technical_approach":"PromptMix is a class-boundary augmentation method for LLM distillation. The likely mechanism is to mix prompts or synthesize intermediate examples that sit closer to decision boundaries, then distill the teacher’s behavior on those harder cases. Compared with standard augmentation, the goal is not generic diversity but targeted boundary coverage. Compared with plain KD, it actively creates high-value training examples instead of passively consuming the original dataset. The main trade-off is realism: if mixed prompts drift too far from natural inputs, the student may fit artificial boundaries.","experiments":"The expected evaluation is on classification-style tasks, comparing standard KD, augmentation-based KD, and PromptMix under limited data and standard settings. Existing heuristics (validation accuracy, perplexity) are either label-hungry or unstable.","importance":"A concrete methodological contribution for prompt selection, and indirectly informative about ICL: it ports the loss-landscape flatness↔generalization story from weight space to prompt space.","technical_approach":"Uses loss-landscape flatness as a proxy for prompt quality: perturb model parameters slightly and measure how much the output distribution changes under each candidate prompt; pick the prompt whose loss surface is flattest. We present an extensive analysis of various benchmarks including a sentence planning benchmark with different granularities. After comparing large language models against state-of-the-start finetuned smaller models, we present a spectrum showing large language models falling behind, are comparable, or exceed the ability of smaller models. However, there is a concern that these models may disclose information in the training data. In this study, we focus on the summarization task and investigate the membership inference (MI) attack: given a sample and black-box access to a model's API, it is possible to determine if the sample was part of the training data. We exploit text similarity and the model's resistance to document modifications as potential MI signals and evaluate their effectiveness on widely used datasets. A Prototypical Network-Based Framework for Built-in Interpretability in Large Language Models","authors":["Sean Xie","Soroush Vosoughi","Saeed Hassanpour"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"Large Language Models (LLMs) have significantly advanced the field of Natural Language Processing (NLP), but their lack of interpretability has been a major concern. Current methods for interpreting LLMs are post hoc, applied after inference time, and have limitations such as their focus on low-level features and lack of explainability at higher level text units. In this work, we introduce proto-lm, a prototypical network-based white-box framework that allows LLMs to learn immediately interpretable embeddings during the fine-tuning stage while maintaining competitive performance. Our method's applicability and interpretability are demonstrated through experiments on a wide range of NLP tasks, and our results indicate a new possibility of creating interpretable models without sacrificing performance. 模块的知识蒸馏到一个共享模块中。关键选择是用低秩分解而非剪枝或量化，因为目标是在训练阶段就控制参数增长，而非事后压缩。与标准 MoE 的区别在于 expert 本身被压缩，而非靠路由稀疏化来省 FLOPs。","experiments":"在多语言机器翻译任务上验证，具体参数量和语言数未在摘要中给出。核心结论是 LMS 在大幅减少参数的同时保持了接近全秩 LS 模块的翻译质量，FD 进一步通过知识融合提升了共享模块的泛化能力。","authors_note":"Haoran Xu, Benjamin Van Durme 等，来自 Johns Hopkins University。Van Durme 是 JHU CLSP 的核心成员，在 NLP/信息抽取领域有长期积累。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Language-specific Can LM-style generative pretraining work on tabular data?","importance":"A paradigm attempt for the tabular domain: a first-shot tabular foundation model via generative pretraining. We include perturbations at multiple levels of abstractions (e.g. lexical perturbations such as typos, and semantic perturbations such as the inclusion of intermediate reasoning steps in the questions) to conduct behavioral analysis on the LLMs. Throughout our experiments, we find that models are more sensitive to certain perturbations such as replacing words with their synonyms.
